review_text:
1- First of all the good stuff... I must say i loved the atmosphere!! There were 2 belly dancers, and 2 singers who performed 👍🏻

2- However the food was very disappointing for the value we paid $75 per person. The food was basically only appetizers!! And we got some fruit, chocolate bar, meghli, and chocolate rolls and rice pudding at the end!! 

3-All lebanese mezzes usually come with a main course either bbq (meat/fish) i was very disappointed will not come back again!! I felt we were ripped off!!! 

3- Seems they are focusing more on the performances going on than the food itself! 

4- The restaurant is kind of small and not sure if they have a ventilation system because the restaurant was filled with white smoke from hooka and cigs. 

I wouldn’t recommend this place to anyone. All restaurants in lebanon have performances with better fixed menus! Which includes a main course!!

address: Monot, Achrafieh Vgr4+7x Beirut, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
Gia la Cucina d'Amore is a hidden gem in the heart of the city. The moment you step inside, you're transported to a charming indoor garden with lush greenery and a relaxing atmosphere. The space is beautifully decorated and makes for a perfect backdrop for a romantic dinner or a night out with friends.

The food is just as impressive as the setting. The menu offers a variety of classic Italian dishes made with fresh, high-quality ingredients. We highly recommend the lasagna and tiramisu - they were both incredibly flavorful and satisfying.

The staff at Gia la Cucina d'Amore are knowledgeable and attentive, making our dining experience all the more enjoyable. Whether you're looking for a special night out or a casual dinner with friends, this restaurant is a must-visit. We can't wait to come back and enjoy the indoor garden atmosphere again!

review_text: Such a lovely discovery which I made!! I was looking for a place to order lunch and I noted the Jars & Co. The menu was interesting and the concept was new. I decided to give it a try. I went for a healthy salad "HAPPY". The name was happy and thats how I felt after eating it. Loved it!! and I do recommend you try it.

address: Roucheid El Dahdah, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
As soon as we stepped inside, the atmosphere was welcoming, and beautifully designed and set the tone to a memorable meal.
The perfect balance between cozy and elegant. The lighting, music, and decor all contributed to a relaxed, enjoyable environment that made us feel right at home.

The food was equally impressive. Every dish was fresh, flavorful, and perfectly cooked. You can really taste the care and quality in each bite. The quinoa and  shrimp was a standout, rich, and beautifully presented and the hummos with herbs was also very delicious.
In the other side, it was my first time tasting the fattet fawaregh, and I was honestly blown away. From the very first bite, the flavors were rich, perfectly balanced, and unexpectedly satisfying. The texture, the seasoning, the aroma, everything came together in the best way. I didn’t expect to love it this much, but it quickly became a new favorite. It was one of those meals you remember long after the last bite.

The staff were friendly and attentive without being intrusive. Overall, a fantastic dining experience. Highly recommended for anyone who appreciates great food in a great setting!

address: Rue Victor Hugo Ashrafieh, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
I am not judging the quality of the food, as I did not have the opportunity to eat there.

However, my experience with this restaurant was extremely disappointing. Their website claims to offer a high-end service, but the reservation form does not work. I tried contacting them via Instagram, email, and WhatsApp, but never received a response.

I then went to the restaurant in person to make a reservation, and a staff member refused to take my booking for two people, claiming there was a minimum of four guests, information that is nowhere indicated. Even worse, the person spoke down to me, suggesting that the $75 menu was probably “a bit expensive” for me.

Verdict: a huge lack of respect and an extremely disappointing service.

address: Monot Street, Beirut 0000 Lebanon
review_title: One of the last still standing nightclubs in Beirut
review_text:
Nestled in one of the smallest streets in Ashrafyeh, Almodovar quickly became one of the busiest pub/night clubs in Beirut. And there’s a reason for this. 
To start with the food is quite interesting with modern fusion Mexican menus and is better than one would expect in a night club. Literally everything is presented with exotic dips that will make your taste buds dance throughout the meal. Also, they have one of the best drinks menus for such a venue. You can order almost any kind of drink or cocktail and you will surely get it. 
However this NOT what makes Almodovar really interesting. The secret is at post 10pm this very particular place transforms into a dancing floor where one can dance to one hearts delight. The music selection is exquisite and on point and the crowds will definitely go wild. THIS is Almodovar’s little secret and it’s a good one. 
Still, a few little details can make or break the mood. For one, some high tables are too small and uncomfortable especially if it’s a group of 3+ pax. Also, on some special nights it is truly overcrowded. Claustrophobic people should avoid. 
Still it is a must do experience. Word of the wise make sure you reserve a good table. My personal recommendation is to try it on a Thursday or Sunday. Both are exceptional nights there. Enjoy!

review_text: We had a pleasant food experience accompanied by good service and attention to details. Dishes were very well served, some better than others. review_text:

Service was great, very professional with a lot of attention to the patrons. Very friendly too.
Prices are on a high however and we opted for cocktails rather than wine because of the prices. Same bottle the night before somewhere else at $45.00 was at $75.00!!!
Still cocktails were not cheap, but this is the way in Beirut for any restaurant that wants itself fancy and trendy.
I had a fish that was nicely and perfectly cooked, very fresh. My wife had a salad and she liked it too.
Recommendable but a bit expensive

review_title: Zawat review ask alot before you order
review_text:
Very disappointed with our recent visit to Zawat. As frequent visitors to Lebanon and long-time patrons of this restaurant, we were shocked to be charged nearly $250 just for fish. The menu listed prices without specifying they were per kilo, the fish wasn’t weighed in front of us, and no price was confirmed before preparing the order. Locals in Saida confirmed we were overcharged — something that feels like we were taken advantage of as foreigners.

To make matters worse, we were charged for parking despite using the restaurant’s own facilities. We’ve dined at many other well-known restaurants across Lebanon, with larger groups and similar meals, and never paid anywhere near this amount.

It’s not about the money — it’s about feeling respected and treated fairly. Sadly, this experience left us feeling scammed and extremely disappointed.

address: Old Saidon Souk Next To The Sea Castle, Sidon Lebanon
review_title: Terrible experience at Saida Rest House
review_text:
Terrible food, terrible service!
I went with my family on the 8th of June 2025, at 9:00 PM, during Eid al-Adha. I understand that the staff might be tired throughout the day and especially in eid but it’s never an excuse to treat customers that way that we were treated!

First, the staff had grim and very grumpy faces, which was super unprofessional! We didn’t say anything, we ordered food and it came quickly ( not surprised since they had barely anyone there except us probably due to their terrible service).

We ordered variety of food from grilled chicken to hot meza, and everything was terrible, anyone that intends to go to such place would expect amazing food and service but both were non existent. We didn’t give them any notes to their food or service we just sat and enjoyed the view.

It all went down to when they placed the check, without us asking for it, but it was fine, it was 11:40 i think. We started dividing the total based on what everyone ordered. They kept coming every 2 minutes asking if the check was ready and we told them that we still need some time until it became 11:50 and the cashier that closes the system came and started shouting at us! At first we were shocked, earlier we asked one of the staff what time do they close he said that when all customers leave, so he didn’t have any right to come barging on us with his terrible attitude demanding to pay the check so he could close the system and go home! That’s your job and like it or not you have to wait, especially for such restaurant. Plus the restaurant says that they close at 12:00 Am so we weren’t passed that time!

And the manager in charge or the one responsible for the reception came and told us that the waiter that told us that they close whenever all costumers leave doesn’t know, which was illogical how would we know that he doesn’t know! His response represents the restaurant so if you wanna blame someone blame your staff and yourself for the ingenuity and unprofessionalism!

We paid and went out, on our way out other customers were leaving too and they also said “m3ash yshofo wejna” which means that they won’t see our faces again probably they had a terrible experience too!

So for anyone that thinks of going there, don’t, it’s not worth the money spent and you could spend way less amount for better food and better service elsewhere. Never again!!

review_text: Appalling. Service non existent and just laughed at a broken glass to serve wine. Portion of calamari for 4 people consistent of 9 rings. I am shocked. The first time I have left a Lebanese restaurant hungry.

address: Salha House, Rachid Nakhle Street, Sur Lebanon
review_text: Although there is a small indoor space, the glory of this restaurant is the outdoor location.  Directly on the sea, the food is fresh and delicious. Served simply, it's best to order local fish.  It's not fancy but it is certainly memorable.

address: Salha House, Rachid Nakhle Street, Sur Lebanon
review_text:
The restaurant is on a platform overlooking the sea: a very good location but not if the wind is up!
Food was fairly priced but uninspiring after Beirut.

address: Al Fanar Street, Tyre 1107 Lebanon
review_title: Nice place with great choice of music
review_text: We celebrated my friend birthday and it was amazing, great choice of music, the stuff are adorable and helpful. If you want to spend a lovely night in TYRE it is the place to be.

address: Al Fanar Street, Tyre 1107 Lebanon
review_title: Great place but too loud music
review_text:
I really enjoy this restaurant, and the service however the only think I didn’t like is that they started playing loud music very early. I went to have dinner and drinks with friends and they started playing loud music around 8:30pm. That is crazy. I couldn’t even talk to my friends at the table. I do hope that they can start playing music later on when people are done with their dinners, but not before. I used to come here a lot before and had great dinners and after party experiences, but this is way too much. I hope the owner implement some changes thinking also about the well-being and harmonious experience of each client, and not just of those who were only want to party. You have a wonderful place to make wonderful memories, but give each clients the opportunity to have a good time without loosing their hearing. 

Thanks for your understanding and I hope you receive my feedbacks well and implement them for the success of your business and the collective health and welfare of your clients. Best wishes,

address: Al Fanar Street, Tyre 1107 Lebanon
review_text:
171/5000

ora basta non so che altro scrivere

address: Al Fanar Street, Tyre 1107 Lebanon
review_title: Cool place for a drink and cool owner
review_text: Great place for a drink at night with great owner, great music and atmosphere a mix of international and lebanese. Great!! keep going on my friend!

address: Port, Tyre, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: The food at Abo Dani is simply fabulous! Super fresh fish and great taste, it really is a very pleasant place. The prices are reasonable and we were warmly welcomed. He remembered us from a visit a year earlier. Definitely our favourite restaurant in Tyr! elyssa and dany

address: Port, Tyre, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: Had a great night, eating A delicious and FRESH Black Grouper cooked by the owner. When there was confusion about our order, they took the unwanted food back, no questions asked. Had a much better meal than at the highly rated Phenicien!

address: Port, Tyre, Tyre Lebanon
review_title: Excellent host, good prices and fish selection top notch
review_text:
Dany does it all at his restaurant: he welcomes you, shows you the day’s catch and helps you choose a fish, then hops into the kitchen and prepares it all. An excellent host, he explained how he has worked at restaurants in Russia, Belarus and Cyprus before coming home to open his own spot. The place is unassuming and that’s a big part of its charm. We had a bottle of wine, a fish to share, salad, 2 mezze and water for $40US. 

We’d recommend.

address: Port, Tyre, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: Amazingg place from the ambiance to the staff to the best food ever ive tasted.Highly recommended especially to sea food loversss ♥️♥️♥️

address: Port, Tyre, Tyre Lebanon
review_text:
Dany is very welcoming and his food is excellent 👌🏻 
We ate some fish or shrimp sandwiches with a very tasty sauce 👏😍

address: Port Entrance st. Qassam square, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: A cute restaurant by the square. It's a family business, the owners are there to greet you and take care of you. Dinner was nice, enjoyed the filet that was recommended to us. It can get quite busy, reserve ahead.

address: Port Entrance st. Qassam square, Tyre Lebanon
review_title: A nice place where to eat in Tyre
review_text:
One of the best places to have dinner in Tyre...
Well know for its steak and meat in general
Don't miss it during your week-end in Tyre

address: Port Entrance st. Qassam square, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: We found the restaurant while checking trip advisor. It was our first time to visit Tyre, the restaurant is small but very cozy, modern but kept their traditional design( traditional Lebanese house). The food is very tasty from fish, shrimps to hummus and batata harra especially batata harra, their recipe is very delicious. As well, owners are very kind, friendly. Thank you for everything.

address: Port Entrance st. Qassam square, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: This is a family owned business, run by the family members. Farida greeted us, very friendly and warm lady. she made us feel very welcomed and we had a little conversation with her. The restaurant is next to Tyre port. Very cozy and romantic place. We ordered the Filet Mignon, which was AMAAAZING. it comes with potatoe Jacket (something I haven't tasted anything like it before) it was so good.  If you ever go to Tyre (sour) you should never miss the opportunity to go there.

address: Port Entrance st. Qassam square, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: The fillet mignon was amazing. Great cozy place and very kind staff! WiFi available and situated in a central place.

address: Old Town Steps Accessible From The Marina Road. Blue Windows, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: Just beside Le Phenicien is Restaurant Tony a slightly shabbier but equally charming option. Tony, the owner of this small  white and blue decorated seafood restaurant, will greet you warmly and fry you up the latest catch.

address: Old Town Steps Accessible From The Marina Road. Blue Windows, Tyre Lebanon
review_text:
Next door to Al Fakhoury and just as easily missed.
I had two excellent, in terms of choice, quality and price, meals there and I would highly recommend it.

address: Old Town Steps Accessible From The Marina Road. Blue Windows, Tyre Lebanon
review_text:
The restaurant is very small with about 4 tables. The owner spends most of his time trying to attract walkers/tourists to see his place. He doesn’t speak any English. It was an Ethiopian lady that was taken all the order and taking care of it. 

Location is very good. You can have sea view and see the fishermen. Then you can park the car in public space and walk around 750 meters or go almost to the restaurant, but than buy a parking ticket of 5,000 LBP. 

Food taste was mixed. Fresh sea bass fish was great! Grilled always. Hummus too oily and same was with baba ganoush. Cheese rolls were of good taste. Service ok. However it lacked a bit of that atmosphere one aims to see at the local place.

address: Old Town Steps Accessible From The Marina Road. Blue Windows, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: We came here twice, to eat the catch of the day. It's a small simple quite cheap venue. But a lot of ambiance.

address: Old Town Steps Accessible From The Marina Road. Blue Windows, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: The owner, Tony, is unprofessional. He didn’t give me my change back. Twice. He got angry when I said I just wanted salad. The view was worth the visit but there are so many restaurants to choose from!! Tyre is incredible, but this restaurant is NOT!

address: Port De Tyr, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: Great service and the food is even better. Order seafood with a few mezes and your day will be perfect. Nicely situated too.

address: Port De Tyr, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: Located right on the harbor, Le Phenicien is one of Tyre's most upscale restaurants. Specializing in fresh seafood and fish, the restaurant is known for its great service, tasty food and amazing view.

address: Port De Tyr, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: We had a lovely dinner of mezze and a sea bream wrapped in lettuce leaves and grilled. Excellent location by the port.  Not to be missed.

address: Port De Tyr, Tyre Lebanon
review_text:
A good choice. A little expensive but the food is good. 
Very busy, it may be a good idea to make a reservation before.

address: Port De Tyr, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: excellent local sublime cuisine fresh fish and Lebanese salads fantastic compliments ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ excellent wine professional and well organized staff compliments

review_title: Zero Customer Respect and Child verbal abusers
review_text: We went there for lunch with our kids. One of their staff decided to mock our kid and curse him out of sudden . Shockingly the management rather than trying to solve the issue they considered what happened as a normal act.

address: Old Souk, Pepe Abed Street Facing historical Castle main gate, Byblos Lebanon
review_text:
Perfect service, beautiful atmosphere and magnificent food. 
Made to feel welcome like family, and and attended to perfectly in every way, the food was beautiful on the eyes and absolutely delicious

address: Old Souk, Pepe Abed Street Facing historical Castle main gate, Byblos Lebanon
review_title: Lunch at Locanda a la Granda in Byblos
review_text: Serving fusion-style cusine and specializing in redesigning famous traditional Lebanese and international dishes, Locanda a la Granda occupies a prime spot in Byblos old souk. Enjoy the view from the terrace while you enjoy the delicious food and restaurant's special l Locanda Beer.

address: Old Souk, Pepe Abed Street Facing historical Castle main gate, Byblos Lebanon
review_text:
So, my family and friends are visiting lebanon l, not for the the first time.  We have been to many places and restaurants,  and everyone was great and nice to us.  

After touring around the area,  we decided to go to this place to have a drink,  eat some appetizers and sweet dishes. When we entered the place,  they didn't say any welcome greetings. We wanted to place an orders,  but after knowing that we don't want to eat main dishes,  he told us that he is "not interested in only serving us if we don't order main dishes". Then he told us to "leave and go somewhere not here" in front of everyone.  Everyone was looking, and it was super embarrassing. 

I will never bring anyone to this place ever. The way we were treated and possibly racially profiled wasn't OK. I will bring my family and friends who are coming to lebanon to better places. 

You can never treat anyone like that ever!!

address: Old Souk, Pepe Abed Street Facing historical Castle main gate, Byblos Lebanon
review_text: Terrible waiter  and customer service  food is good but overall bad experience ruined the evening  we will never recommend it

address: Jbeil, Byblos Lebanon
review_text:
It is a lovely cafe, perfect for gatherings with your friends. 

Comfy sofas, a cup of coffee and you feel liken you are in New York sitting in  central perk, waiting for Chandler or Joey from Friends to open the door. 

Also a lovely place for read, write or study.

address: Jbeil, Byblos Lebanon
review_text: Visited the place a week ago. It was quite nice, with indoor and outdoor seating which is great. Smoking is allowed outside. The coffee is good and the ambience is very chilled. Loved the music a lot great oldies era. You can take a stroll in Jbeil after your cup of coffee. Recommended 👌

address: Rue Pepe Abed, Byblos Lebanon
review_text: I got a very bad experience!! Im a tourist from kuwait i went there to have a dinner with the family the girl who is working their was very ruude with us when we enter the restaurant without any reason!! and we decide to left the place without ordering anything .

address: Rue Pepe Abed, Byblos Lebanon
review_text:
Feniquia is exceptional.
Amazing service with incredibly warm and professional staff, extraordinary food full of flavor and passion, and a beautiful atmosphere that makes the whole experience unforgettable. Truly one of the best restaurants — highly recommended.

address: Rue Pepe Abed, Byblos Lebanon
review_text:
Very dry grill not good quality food and meat good starter but not good libanese food service bad 
To hot inside not enough fan for everyone

address: Rue Pepe Abed, Byblos Lebanon
review_title: Horrible service. Food came wrong or very late.
review_text: Very poor experience. One third of our orders came out wrong and had to be returned to be redone. It took 45 minutes to make baked potatoes. We waited for an hour after most of us were done eating for a shawarma sandwich order for my teenage son. Eventually, after many “3 more minutes madame”, I sent the boys with my husband to have a tawook sandwich at a restaurant next door. All this, with the restaurant mostly empty..Horrible service. Horrible experience.

address: Rue Pepe Abed, Byblos Lebanon
review_text: Nicely presented plates either hunging from the ceiling or served on charcoal, big variety to chose, freshly cooked and a nice surprise at the end: on the house after 8 flavoured ice cream. Shots of arak served for all also complimentary

address: Edde Yard Old Souk, Byblos Lebanon
review_text:

The steak nothing special and cold and not the way we asked to be cooked

address: Edde Yard Old Souk, Byblos Lebanon
review_title: eCafe, in the center of Byblos, has Mediterranean atmosphere and delicious international menu.
review_text: ECafe has a friendly and helpful chef and staff who provide  delicious meals with Middle Eastern flair. I spent a delightful Thanksgiving dinner in the private dining room.

address: Edde Yard Old Souk, Byblos Lebanon
review_title: Beautiful setting with European/North American food
review_text: We only had drinks here after a stroll through the Souks so I can’t speak to the food - but the setting is truly lovely and refreshingly quiet after the bustling shops and boiling hot castle. Lovely for a pit-stop.

address: Edde Yard Old Souk, Byblos Lebanon
review_title: Not what you expect but in a nice way
review_text:
Byblos is full of Lebanese cuisine and this is completely different. The speciality is really BBQ steaks and grills of all sorts along with potatoes and salads. In a sense more North American or European style.

Excellent quality steaks, cooked on a wood fired BBQ to perfection along with roasted potatoes, salads and a delicious mushroom sauce to go with the steak.

Also a very good selection of Lebanese wines. The Reds go well with the steaks.

Deserts were good as well, especially the fresh waffles, chocolate ice cream and strawberries.

address: Edde Yard Old Souk, Byblos Lebanon
review_text: The place is located in the heart of the Souk. The overall experience is great. The food was so delicious, the staff is friendly and helpful. They offered us desserts because they forgot to bring our salad. That was a nice gesture!

address: Le Phoenix De Byblos Public Beach, Byblos 000000 Lebanon
review_text: Great seaside location. The best music vibe, service, yummy drinks and munchies. Reasonably priced. Unforgettable sunset views. Just delightful.

address: Rue 30 old town byblos Old Souk, Byblos Lebanon
review_text:
The place is nice having a nice music and great DJ.
People are friendly there.
You can enjoy any kind of drink or cocktail.
They have a good and tasty house wine.

address: Rue 30 old town byblos Old Souk, Byblos Lebanon
review_text:
A nice and casual place in the heart of Byblos.
People are friendly there, you can enjoy the music, a fusion & mix between arabic and english. The ambiance is nice.
They have a good house wine.

address: Rue 30 old town byblos Old Souk, Byblos Lebanon
review_text: My favorite destination when in lebanon! Their bartender is one of the best ive ever met! Must try!!

address: Rue 30 old town byblos Old Souk, Byblos Lebanon
review_text: We we're walking around and decided to stop for a drink. We were immediately welcomed by bartender and the owner of the place. They told us a bit about the area and served the drinks. They didn't have exactly what I asked however they came up with alternative which was highly appreciated! Unfortunately we didn't visit the place in the evening but I'm sure they have fantastic ambience during the night hours by having DJ playing. Great location, heartfelt experience that worth to be tried!!! We will definitely come back 😊

address: Main Old Souk road Old Souk, Byblos 7111 Lebanon
review_text: Very friendly atmosphere, quality spirits and cocktails, cold beer, on ressonable price . Paul is there to welcome you.

address: Byblos Lebanon
review_text:
The food is excellent, the pulled half chicken tasted great but it was over burned, also the shush Tawook, same thing,
We ordered some appetizers,
We got everything as it should, but the grilled cheese rolls, came too late, after we nearly finished our main course.
And they refused to take it back, claiming that they had other orders on the grill. This was not very nice.
At the end they brought us some nice ice cream as a complimentary dessert,
Overall experience was good.
To be repeated often

address: Mestita Highway, Byblos Lebanon
review_text:
I loved the idea of having three restaurants in one place, you can order whatever you want from three different menus. 
We ordered a pizza, a shawarma and a burger, all of them were so delicious!
I also loved the ambiance and the decoration.
I recommend everyone to visit this place!

address: Jbeil, Cooperative Of Fishermen And Fisherwoman, Byblos Lebanon

address: Main Street, Bikfaya Lebanon
review_text:
Went with friends. To the spot they've taken In LOLA in al NAAS for the summer months. 

Nice garden seating with Pizza Oven in corner. 

Ordered 

Margherita Pizza. Made fresh in front of us and tasted great. 

Truffle Gnocchi. Very good and perfectly cooked. 

Edamame. Biggest portion I have ever seen served. Lol

Bruschetta which was great and fresh. 

Burrata which was cold and fresh and great. 

I wanted the Salmon Tartare but unfortunately it was not available that night. 

And Pain Per Du for dessert. One of the best I have had. 

Will return. 
Will recommend. 

Thx Guys.

address: Main Street, Bikfaya Lebanon
review_text: Its the best italian restaurant in lebanon,the risoto au pesto is the best risoto i've ever tasted in my life

address: Main Street, Bsalim 22439 Lebanon

address: Main Street, Bsalim 22439 Lebanon
review_text:
Delicious food! it's a must try restaurant :)

address: Saint-Martin-Saint-Charbel Road, Aannaya Lebanon
review_text:
we were a group, upon arrival the Hostess who seated us, did not smile, as we were trying to choose from the menu we got confused, she started to show her impatience, she should have said please take your time and I will come back to you. 
We ordered the food, and to our shock, the main dishes like grilled mix plate was so small that it wouldn't feed a 10-year-old kid, the plate was $16. Everything served to us were in tiny portions. Some of what we ordered did not come on time because she forgot to place it with the kitchen, not even an apology! 

We walked out of the restaurant and had agreed that it is our first and last time to come to this Ayloul Restaurant. We do not recommend this place at all.

address: Seaside road, Byblos 1401 Lebanon
Elysee Street Centre el harf, Byblos Lebanon
Al Ramel Street Facing Edde Sands Hotel, Byblos Lebanon
review_text: One can enjoy a vast array of Lebanese food items and fresh sea food specialties with the most beautiful view and ambience. Prices, service , staff and hygiene are also highly amazing .

address: Al Ramel Street Facing Edde Sands Hotel, Byblos Lebanon
review_text: The view is very nice and the place is very nice , we tried many things  the calamare, homos bi har and regular , mtabal, mashawi are great. I didn't like the potato salad( mahrousseh) and the birds at all . The service is a bit slow but ok . The one that i like the most is the knafe woww so good. The special hookah is really nice ... overall this restaurant is recommended!

my favourite dish is the small fish -bezre

address: Al Ramel Street Facing Edde Sands Hotel, Byblos Lebanon

review_text:
The food is unfortunately not very satisfying. The meat is chewy and overcooked for lamb, some of the mezze (octopus with provincial sauce) is just over spiced and has extra lemon in it, you loose the initial taste of it, the fwerigh with tomato sauce were tasteless. Hummos and salad were average. Service is slow although we were the only customers at that time. And of course you ask for something and get something else kind of mistakes. 
This venue has a lot of improvements to make

address: Al Ramel Street Facing Edde Sands Hotel, Byblos Lebanon

review_text: amazing owners, friendly professional view, very tasty fish fusion menu, wonderful view, reasonable prices, casual seating, suitable for  events... in short: you will always come back!!

address: Byblos Lebanon, Byblos Lebanon
review_text: The best beachfront restaurant in lebanon with amazing view and great food.we went there for lunch and the service was outstanding. I recommend everyone to try it

address: Byblos Lebanon, Byblos Lebanon

address: Nalu Amchit, Jbail Lebanon
Byblos Sud, Byblos Lebanon
Byblos, Mastita Seaside Road Bint El Chalabiya Building, Byblos 4507 Lebanon
review_text: Great food and good service. It is a newly opened place so the servers weren’t that great with knowing what was on the menu but they did a good job even though there was a lot of people and had a lot to do.

address: Voie 13, Centre Laura GF, near Byblos bank Facing Salameh, Byblos Lebanon
review_title: One of the best organic shops and cafés in Lebanon
review_text: I’m always looking for nice places to work wherever I go, comfortable to sit and hang-out, not too noisy, naturally well-lit, with delicious coffee and food if possible... I’m glad I’ve found the perfect place when I’m in Byblos, Lebanon: OSOM Organic Café and Shop. It’s so peaceful, with zen music and lemongrass incense smell, comfortable sitting area, delicious carrot cake and brownies, lots of vegan options, fresh lemonade and carrot juice, all organic and at an affordable price... The perfect place for freelancers to work, eat, and even shop.

address: Voie 13, Centre Laura GF, near Byblos bank Facing Salameh, Byblos Lebanon
review_title: An outdoor pet friendly coffee, organic place, in jbeil...
review_text: In the heart of a city, you ll find a little peaceful garden, with everything healthy, that u imagine.. Beautiful music and the great smile of the owner... A place to visit, and enjoy..

address: Voie 13, Centre Laura GF, near Byblos bank Facing Salameh, Byblos Lebanon
review_text:
Amazingly decorated shop, with various organic products. 
My favourite spots are the seating area outside and the couch for reading or working upstairs.
Friendly and helpful staff.

address: Byblos, Byblos 961 Lebanon
review_text: A decent place for low cost food with great taste. The menu is limited to fast made food, however tasteful and you are not hungry when you leave.

address: Byblos, Byblos 961 Lebanon
review_text:
We checked out this place following a recommendation by locals. It’s very close to the main touristy area of Byblos(Jbeil), but remains off the beaten path. If not walking there from the main souk and sites, the Uber ride is an affordable 4$! It’s along the main road to the Saint Charbel Monastery.

This is where the locals go for fresh, clean, affordable and tasty Lebanese food. We were two and split the fattouch (delicious), hummus, and the mixed grill. Everything is of top quality and the soujouk kebabs were particularly excellent. 

We would definitely come back and recommend Kabablaki as a place to check out and eat where locals eat.

We ate at the restaurant and it seemed to attract a lot of delivery orders (takeaway).  I  believe they have convenient hours since it stays open late.

address: Byblos, Byblos 961 Lebanon
review_text:
Ordered a delivery of some grilled chicken and a tabboule: the delivery arrived in 45 minutes, the food was in adequate temperature, the portions are generous and the prices are good. Overall recommended.
PS: Tastiest garlic paste I've had in a while

address: Byblos, Byblos 961 Lebanon
review_text: If you’re looking for a place to try out Lebanese food/grill then Kabablaki is the best place in Jbeil!! Very friendly service, yummy food and fair prices

address: Byblos, Byblos 961 Lebanon
review_title: Great lebanese barbecue with good prices
review_text: The restaurant is such a nice one to visit in Jbeil area in order to have a delicious Lebanese meal with very fair prices!

address: Crossroad 13th Avenue, Byblos Lebanon
review_text:

Service:5/5

address: Crossroad 13th Avenue, Byblos Lebanon
review_text: It was disappointing terrible as we ordered 5 plates 4 of them arrived on time and the fish plate arrived after 55 minutes and the filet suppose to be grilled i don't know why they did but it looks like they made it in the microwave or sauted it was really bad as the grilled fish filet was for my mother and we ordered more bread and never arrived ,not even a manager shows to apologize or to make up for the incident that happened .it is really disappointing and not recommended

Cold food

address: Crossroad 13th Avenue, Byblos Lebanon

address: Crossroad 13th Avenue, Byblos Lebanon
review_text: The food is excellent... Service is excellent... Shisha is well prepared and served... They serve their branded arak which taste good...

address: 13 Voie, Byblos Lebanon
review_text: We enjoyed a lovely dinner at this restaurant in Achrafieh , Beirut . The food was delicious , the service was good and the ambiance is great.

address: 13 Voie, Byblos Lebanon
review_text: Romantic and cozy place where one can enjoy a variety of French food items are the most reasonable prices , prompt service, friendly staff and optimal hygienic conditions.

address: 13 Voie, Byblos Lebanon
review_text: I recommend this restaurant in Jbeil Byblos, for it’s nice ambiance, old stones, wine cave and for the great staff they have, such welcoming and always ready to serve with a big smile! The Quiche Lorraine is not bad at all , especially when it’s warm, nice salads , duck and steaks. Good choice of local and foreign wines at a good price. Ideal for romantic dinner or friends dinner.

address: 13 Voie, Byblos Lebanon
review_text:
The services and the atmosphere were very good and cheerfully welcomed by the hostess, there is a cosy atmosphere that reigns there and we liked it. 
We asked for their speciality burger which was damn good and one the tastiest. I recommend this place.

address: 13 Voie, Byblos Lebanon
review_text:
Fin Faim is french; and it's generous! 
It serves a variety of well cooked food; it is cozy and still is nice for a family gathering. Make sure you get some extra money and buy yourself a bottle of wine; cause it tastes yummm!

address: Main Street, Byblos Lebanon
review_text:
Lovely modern clean restaurant with fantastic service 
Staff are happy and super obliging 
Food is really good, tasty and big portions as well as reasonable price 
Two visits, had shawarma, pizza, dessert, all awesome
Shout out to Mohamed Alsheakh, awesome young man who looked after us each time

address: Main Street, Byblos Lebanon
review_title: Tasty food just outside of the souk
review_text:
The restaurant is just outside the souk and very popular with local families..the decor is not great and most waiters only speak Arabic. However the food is very fresh and tasty. Chicken skewer was perfectly cooked, juicy and not dry.. price is fair.
Overall it’s a great alternative to all the overpriced tourist restaurants serving bland food in the souk

address: Main Street, Byblos Lebanon
review_text: This place is for you to grab your meal quickly. It is always crowded because it has really good food. But the dessert is much better than normal food!

address: Main Street, Byblos Lebanon
review_title: I have been eating here for over 15 years
review_text: Very good food, good service, kind staff, not expensive. Only good things to say.  They have inside A/C seating and outside seating. Their ice cream is amazing. Their fruit cocktails are VERY good. I love the pizza, the salads, the chawarma...

address: Main Street, Byblos Lebanon
review_title: Check the food before delivering it
review_text: I used to eat Chicken shawarma almost every week from this restaurant. I ordered last week a delivery for me and my friends ( I advised them to order from there) and it was really terrible, they couldn’t eat their sandwiches. Really disappointed

address: Mastita - Drive Thru, Byblos Lebanon
review_text:

address: Jbeil - Aannaya Road, Byblos Lebanon
review_text:
Ksaar (for me and my family) is an annual must have dining every year we vacation in Lebanon.

The cuisine is plentiful with a wide variety of choices from Lebanese / Italian & generally Mediterranean options including grilled and seafood platters.

Kids menus are also available with a bonus playground onsite and monitored by restaurant staff, however, parental or nanny supervision is highly recommended for 
young children till the age of 7 in my opinion.

The local delicacies such as sujok and mhamraa, and kubbeh are fantastic with a glass of beer ;) Pizza's, fries and burgers are also available along with pasta  

Ksaar also has outdoor seating for those with a need of a hooka bubbly.

I recommend Ksaar and rate it highly.

address: Port Street, Byblos Lebanon
review_title: undoubtedly the best fatoush of my life so far
review_text: We stumbled on this place due to their signs by the roadside. What luck and what a revelation. We had a set meal: the hummus was perfect, the babaganoush was to die for. Because we were wondering about it, we were shown how they smoked the aubergine. The fatoush was just incredible and contained such a variety of herbs leaves, some of which we didn't recognise. A perfect balance. Then long double cooked chips, calamari which was cleanly crisp outside and succulent tasty with no rubberiness. It was served with a variant of tartare sauce, pleasantly acidic. Then we had a variety of crisply fried smallish fish. Utterly delicious, too much for me. When we thought this was over, they brought us a delicious, crisp fried sweet pie, like a rose water flavoured Greek galactoburico. We were in heaven. it is all fresh, much of it home produce, the owners are fishermen. It cost less than half the price of restaurants on the harbour, yet this is just yards from the sea and so friendly.  One of those finds I would not tell you about if I lived more locally!

address: Port Street, Byblos Lebanon
review_text: Our driver bought us here for lunch as he knew the owners. The restaurant was empty but clean and well maintained. They put on a fabulous spread of mezze and fresh seafood. (They didn’t give us a menu the driver asked them to bring us a range) Everything was fresh, well seasoned and delicious. The bill was $90 total for three of us with soft drinks but we had so much food we could justify the price. Thank you Amal for cooking such a lovely home cooked meal.

address: Port Street, Byblos Lebanon

review_text:
Just to tell you that before working in taxi business I was a restaurant director for 12 years).

I tried before this restaurant and it was one of my preference. 3 weeks ago I decided to invite someone for a small lunch ... because we have a business meeting after one hour .
My friend asked for a pizza and one beverage and I asked for shish taouk on tabliyeh ... 20mn later my friend got his pizza ... and me ??? nothing.
Before finishing his pizza , my friend proposed me to eat the last slide of the pizza ... I did because I was hungry... What about my order : Nothing..
Guys... Normally when  we  2 persons enter a restaurant to eat, they shall eat together ... right?
As I learned during my career that the Client is a KING.. In this case the KING became ... ?
.... I told them that I don't need anymore my order.. because the business meeting is in 10 mn .. and I asked for the bill ...
The director or maybe the owner* bring the bill without any apologies.... This is the KING that I am
... and this is the professionalism that he is ..
I paid with pleasure... but the note if I can put is  - 10  .. and NEVER AGAIN...

address: Seaside Road Jbeil District, Amchit Lebanon
review_title: Rude owner, careless, insulting and a bully
review_text:
My partner and I like to sit next to each other so we talk quietly and share a nice experience. The owner approached us towards the end of our lunch and told us change our seating habit, this is after we finished eating and are just chilling enjoying our time. We were taken by that comment and chose to leave a few moments later. There were 4 men behind us downing scotch, talking loud on their phones while eating, nothing wa said about them. We paid and left but in retrospect lots of actions could have taken. Instead of making a scene, we called the owner on the way asking for reasons for his tone, he said "it's my restaurant and I can do as I chose, and for us not to come back again, hangs up the phone" 

Could be an identity crisis issue he is facing  but if u ask me and you are a respected client, specially a woman, the owner may have some issues...

A time like this when we support our local businesses, only to be insulted, disrespected, and humiliated in front of others with no real reasons given, I can only phathom why some men behave this way towards women in this day and age.

address: Seaside Road Jbeil District, Amchit Lebanon
review_title: A gem seafood restaurant in Amchit
review_text: This is a very small restaurant that is part of a fishery on the coast of Amchit north of Byblos. The fishery is well known under the name of its owner Pierre Abboud but few people know that it is also a restaurant. The place is very small (8 tables only) and there is no decoration. Actually you can’t even see it from the road because it is hidden by a tree. But the food is so good. Of course, since Pierre is a fisherman you get the catch of the day. All fresh and perfectly cooked  and seasoned. We had a selection of calamari with garlic and parsley, fried fish and broiled sole. Accompanied by hummus and fattoush. It was all delicious. Service is fast. And the surprise: prices are way cheaper than the trove of fancy seafood restaurants dotting the Byblos-Amchit coast. Definitely a hidden gem.

Their customer service was extremely unprofessional, and to make things worse, we were still charged for the dish! This is not how a restaurant should treat its customers. address: Main Street, Byblos Lebanon
review_text:
As a Byblos resident living 5 min away from Mon Maki, I would caution against visiting this restaurant due to the alarming inconsistency in their food quality. The last time I ordered for pick-up, the sushi was horrible, and I'm not exaggerating when I say "horrible". Specifically, I ordered sushi rolls that included salmon, mango, and avocado. The mangos and avocados were not ripe at all. I'm talking apple textured mangos and rubber textured avocados. It's unbelievable that they could even have the audacity to serve them like that. Almost as if it was done on purpose. And to top it off, the rice was also undercooked, resulting in a horrible overall experience. We were 3 people and we couldn't eat more than two pieces.
I don't usually write reviews for restaurants, but with their ridiculous prices, I felt the responsibility to share my honest experience and warn other people. Especially after reaching out to the restaurant to express my concerns, and all they did was reply to me with "we'll call you back in a bit" (they never did btw).

review_text: We had a dinner for two me and my wife, the service was excellent, we were welcomed first by a guy name Mazen and he was extraordinary & generous when he offered complimentary   fresh lemonade , later we served the food by a very warm waitress names Clarita who was helpful and attentive in every detail, the lasagna was out of this world (oven baked) , the margarita pizza was yummy as well , but the icing on the cake was the tiramisu 🤤 a must try hidden gem when you visit the area

address: Mraba'a Street, Byblos Lebanon
review_title: Hats off, from a usually fussy client!
review_text: The overall best place in Byblos if you like authentic, fresh and delicious Italian cuisine. This is the WOW place, I would say. Aesthetically a joy for the senses. The beautiful decor, the comfortable chairs, a cozy lighting and a busy open kitchen! You would commit a sin if during your stay in Byblos you don't have at least one meal here. I had a delicious freshly made Tiramisu here with an ice Americano. The Tiramisu is a one way ticket to heaven! Then I spotted a waiter grinding fresh pesto to go with freshly made bread, another work of magic. Highly attentive, courteous yet professional servers. It was delightful to see a number of tables celebrating reunions inside and on the outside patio of the restaurant. A kids' cubby house with a slide would keep parents happy and kids safe while healthy and fresh food is served. I wish I was staying longer in Byblos to sample their entire menu. Hats off, from a usually fussy client!

review_text: This is a hidden gem once you discover you'll become a regular there! The place is tucked away between EL Mina buildings on Groupie roundabout so you will need to ask around if this is your first time. Trust me, it's worth it! For around 30 USD you will get a set menu of all kinds of mezza dishes plus the catch of the day. The place does not have shisha but they will accommodate your request if you would like one. If you are a large group of 20, you can request that the whole place be booked for you only. You can purchase alcohol and play whatever music you would like. The place also has a TV and AC's for when it's too cold/hot.

address: Naccach Main Road Khoury Center, Antelias Lebanon
review_text: The best way to describe this place is as a Lebanese version/parallel to what American Diners are repackaged as for the London/UK market. The layout of the place would not suggest this necessarily, but the design of the menu is very much American Diner in London, and the food takes on a similar concept. Quick food that has an indulgent quality. The ingredients certainly taste and come across as high quality. Very clean, not greasy. The portions are moderate, I could have done with a bit more fries with my mixed grill. All very tasty though and a non pretentious exploration and celebration of both Armenian and Lebanese cuisines, successfully executed. Somewhere you can stop off and have a quick bite, or sit upstairs and relax. There are a lot of interesting things on the menu to choose from and I'll definitely go back to try some of the things I didn't get to order, it was difficult choosing! A progressive attempt at modernising traditional dishes without aiming for a high end market. Easy going, non committal, just go and enjoy. They also have a lager brewed locally from one of Lebanon's new microbreweries, worth a try!

address: Naccach Main Road Khoury Center, Antelias Lebanon
review_text: Very welcoming staff, great choice of Armenian/Lebanese mix food. Ordered the very famous Angus beef Abaw burger, the very tasteful spinach mante (an Armenian specialty) and a marinated chicken plate with special sauce for my son. All portions are generous and everything is tasty. Don't miss the french fries with their magic herbs mixture. Place is clean, everything is cooked in front of you.I loved the concept. If you want to experience tasty Armenian and Lebanese cuisine at reasonable prices, it's the place to go.

review_title: Mama Food at its best in Jounieh
review_text: I have visited Darseen Restaurant for lunch with a relative on recommendation . The restaurant open for lunch till very late and  serves Iraqi and Lebanese food . The owner lady is the chief . The place is small but cheerful with out door seating . Very clean , The owner is chatty and she can advice you if you don’t sure what you want to order . I have ordered Sheik Mahshi  ( egg plant stuffed with meat and herbs in tomato sauce ) and Rice . It’s was so tasty and the taste of it has reminded me of my late mother cooking.  My relative ordered Kusi ( lamb meat cooked with herbs and its natural sauce ) . He enjoys it so much . The restaurant serves wine , limited choices but was an excellent Lebanese red wine . We are planing to revisit today for their day plate , Pacha ( special Iraqi dish of lamb) I Strongly recommend this restaurant . Paying a visit is a must if you are staying or visiting Jounieh during lunch time. A visit you will never regret

The star of our visit was a dense, luscious chocolaty hot mess topped with a thick homemade whipped cream. You must try the chocolat chaud viennois artisanal. You will not be disappointed!

address: Kaslik Tadros Building, Jounieh Lebanon
review_text:
Fleur Thé Menu, Reviews, Photos, Location and Info - Zomato
In a time where commercial spots are invading us, authentic concepts, no matter how rare, are to be cherished!
Clever are the owners who seized the idea that people are coming not to fill their stomach but their eyes, souls, and hearts with exquisite experiences.
On a day where the weather was confused, I stepped, like an Alice in a wonderland of my own.
054
044035
Fleur Thé, a resto/coffee/ flower shop, and yes Juliana mastered it all. Inside the "garden" nestles a cottage delicately decorated by the owner, herself an Interior architect, from items collected during her travels. You can also get your hands on paintings of a talented Iranian painter.
053040
Juliana literary pampers her "visitors" with full attentive service. You have to witness it to know what I am talking about.
We opted for a fresh light lunch, and so we picked from the versatile menu:
- Pousses de Betteraves: circles of goat cheese, imported from France, creamy texture melting in your mouth over a bed of Rocca, slices of beetroot, artichokes, grapefruit and grilled mushrooms coated in a citrusy sauce, tasty as ever
077
- Roulades de thon: fresh tuna rolled in nori papers and carefully placed over Rocca papers and mango chutney, Jocelyne the chef is really creative!
068
- Catered to my vegetarian friend's taste, Juliana, caring as ever, made her a grilled veggies club sandwich with a spread of  homemade guacamole and a side of roasted rosemary potatoes, ah delicioussss
072
- For the dessert, the temptations are many, yet we opted for the fresh out of the oven flourless chocolate cake with a side of homemade cream. Julianna offered us the rich dense hot chocolate made out of premium chocolate, DELICACY;)
005
I highly recommend you all to go get pampered and try what Fleur thé has to offer. Not only that, Juliana organizes workshops too!!

address: Jounieh El Chir, Port Street, Jounieh Lebanon
review_text:
We were in Greece before coming to Lebanon for a visit. We thought we will never eat as good ice cream as the ones we got at Waflehouse in Vouliagmeni(Athens). We were soooo wrong. This is an amazing place that has the most delicious  ice cream. I have never tasted anything as good as that. It is a family business that has been inherited from father to son and grand son since 1925. The place offers several tables to sit outside for enjoying the ice cream while watching people driving by showing off their cars😊. We took several kind, plain ice cream with different taste as is and some arrangement that the shop offer...and the rooooools, something I ve never tried before, the kids loved it. Not to mention how surprised I was to find so many sorbet choices they had. The lemon/chocolate/mastikka will take you to heaven😃
The owner is very kind and speak both English and French as he is more than happy to give you as well some details about the background of his piece of history that he owns. Strongly recommended.

address: Jounieh old Souk seaside road just before ATCL, Jounieh Lebanon
review_text:
Usually, we go to the one in Amchit but we decided to try the one in Jounieh. 
I understand that the circumstances aren't great, but if you're going to be open, at least make an effort. Not a single smile on any of the staff.
Food service was slow, and there were only two tables. No napkins provided - had to ask. Hummus and baba ghanoush were average as was the fattoush. Lemonade lacked sugar. Cheese rolls were double fried and cold. Batata kizibra did not taste fresh. Chich taouk was dry and stuck to the skewer. Served on a half ripped (like I do at home) piece of bread. For dessert, fruit wasn't fresh. Had to ask for ice. They brought it. Took it away. And I asked for a back again. Etc. It was one thing after another and I just wanted to get out of there so I asked for the check which took at least 20 minutes to arrive. 
They would not accept a credit card so I gave them a $100 bill for a $55 lunch.  The woman said she didn't have change so she requested something smaller but if I didn't have it, she'd have to go get change. This was not said in a very nice way. So she proceeded to stand at the end of the table while I counted out enough dollars/Lebanese money to pay. After she took the check, they stood around, counting it to see how much I had left for a tip. Tacky. On the way out, I passed multiple employees, but nobody said a word. 
If I'm going to spend this much money, I can go anywhere else and get treated better and have fresh food!! Very disappointed. Won't be back!

address: Jounieh old Souk seaside road just before ATCL, Jounieh Lebanon
review_text:
Unbelievably disappointing.. Bad management, poor service.. The worst experience ever is an understatement..
First, let's start by the atmosphere. We had our baptism lunch there. The air conditioners were turned on an hour before our arrival (I knew this from the decoration team that I've hired) which was not enough to cool the huge space so it was scorching. No music to create a great ambiance.
Second, I've never experienced a service as poor as this one. But no wonder why it was that poor when we re dealing with unprofessional management. Lack of communication and organization. We ordered the premium set menu but some items weren't served in the appropriate quantity and we didn't get any dessert.
The baptism cake wasn't left in a cooled place before being served which has ruined this moment.
If you want your special gatherings and moments to be dramatically ruined, visit this restaurant.

address: Kaslik, Jouneh Road, Jounieh Lebanon
review_text:
I had one of the worst dining experiences ever at La Crêperie on Valentine’s Day.

We made a reservation in advance, and the restaurant required payment upfront. When we arrived, things were already disorganized—we had to wait in line for around 20 minutes just to be seated.

Once seated, the service was extremely poor. We repeatedly asked the waiter for drinks, but they were either delayed or never properly served. Even something as simple as water was handled oddly—the waiter would just fill our glasses instead of leaving the bottle at the table.

The biggest issue: after waiting three hours, no food was served at all. We kept following up with staff, but our concerns were ignored. Eventually, we left without having dinner. We weren’t the only ones—at least 10 other couples also walked out hungry and frustrated.

Despite multiple complaints, the staff showed no concern. Since they had already taken payment in advance, it felt like customer satisfaction simply didn’t matter to them.

This was a very disappointing and frustrating experience, especially for a special occasion like Valentine’s Day. I would strongly advise others to avoid this place.

The food service was extremely slow and the portions were so little. Some of our guests left without getting the chance to eat the main course because it was served so late. They are understaffed and their operations are a mess. I do not recommend this place for my worst enemy! Do not go there.

address: Makaad El Mir, Batroun Lebanon
review_title: Disappointing Dining Experience at Chez Maguy
review_text:
Our visit to Chez Maguy in Batroun was a letdown from start to finish. The tabbouleh was overwhelmingly salty, the batata harra and fried fish were undercooked, and the grilled shrimp hadn’t been cleaned, leaving us to do it ourselves at the table. On top of that, the service was frustrating—an older gentleman from the staff took our salad plate before we were done and insisted it was finished, despite our objection.

We also ordered a grilled fish based on their recommendation, and while the choice itself was good, we were shocked when the bill arrived—$40 for a single piece of fish. Between the poorly prepared food, the dismissive service, and the unexpected price tag, this was not an experience I’d want to repeat or recommend.

address: Bahsa, Batroun Lebanon
review_text:
I had one of the worst dining experiences ever at Swiss Butter in Batroun. The food was mediocre at best, but the real problem was the dishonest way they handle orders. The waitress recommended a ribeye that wasn’t even on the menu and never told us the price — which turned out to be a shocking $55 per plate. We ordered it twice, still never being informed of the cost, only to find out at the end when the bill came. This is deceptive, unethical, and unacceptable. Any restaurant with integrity tells customers the price before selling something, especially when it’s outrageously overpriced. The service was disappointing, the experience left a bad taste in every way, and I will warn others so they don’t get ripped off the way we did.

address: Mina street, Batroun Lebanon
review_text:
This seafood restaurant stood out as a culinary revelation that defied our expectations. Despite initially being overshadowed by a couple of other higher-ranked seafood restaurants in the area, we decided to give Bistr’eau a try as our first meal, upon arriving in Batroun. Little did we know, it would turn out to be the culinary highlight of our stay, ultimately the place we selected as our last meal and the grand finale of our time in Batroun.

Situated beneath a series of picturesque succession of ornate arches along the waterfront, within one of Batroun's historical buildings at the port of the Batroûn Old Souk, this restaurant stood out as a true outlier. What made this dining experience truly exceptional was the symphony of flavors orchestrated by the fusion of cuisines, the inventive pairing of traditional and rarely-used ingredients, and the meticulous execution of each dish. Between our two visits, we embarked on a culinary journey through almost the entire menu, relishing every morsel.

The appetizers alone were a testament to the culinary prowess at play – from the refreshing Fattouch to the innovative Hummus with shrimp, the smoky allure of the aubergine with sundried tomato tarator, and fresh zaatar. The culinary exploration continued with Grilled Calamari, Sicilian octopus, shrimp fokhara with tomato sauce, and perfectly roasted Patatas Bravas. All this food only served to pave the way for the triumphant main courses—seafood risotto and the pièce de résistance, a perfectly seasoned deep-fried whole fish. The culinary symphony reached its crescendo with the Pain Perdu à la mode, offering the perfect sweet note to conclude our culinary odyssey.

review_title: One of the best in Lebanon
review_text:
One of my rituals Every time I visit Lebanon,  is to eat at this restaurant.  The food is authentic home prepared,  very tasty with good clean organic ingredients. Their salads are garden fresh, their appetizers are truley appetizing,  and their meats are very fresh and scrumptuous. There is an indoor section which is great for the winter time,  and sn outdoor section which is absolutely beautiful. The Lebanese mountain village ambiance is pronounced in the building it occupies,  and the outdoor garden like setting, complete with a beautiful spring stream which passes at the restaurant borders,  with a small walking bridge to cross into the restaurant . The service is impeccable,  with the owners always at hand making sure all goes well. I will continue to cone to this restaurant as we have become friends with the owner,  and will always cherish their hospitality. 
They also have a venue for private occasions in the scenic lower section of the place. Highly recommended.

review_text:
Bamboo Tripoli is an absolute gem for Asian cuisine enthusiasts! From the moment we stepped in, we were enveloped in a warm and inviting ambiance. The service was impeccable—attentive, friendly, and genuinely welcoming, ensuring every detail of our experience was perfect.

The food was nothing short of extraordinary. We began with the Dynamite Shrimp and Crab Salad—both starters were bursting with flavor. The shrimp had the perfect balance of spice and crunch, while the salad was refreshing and beautifully presented. For the mains, the Sweet and Sour dish was a delightful harmony of tangy and sweet flavors, and the Mongolian Beef was a standout, with tender slices of beef in a rich, savory sauce that left us craving more.

To finish, we indulged in the Cookies Pie for dessert—a sublime ending to a flawless meal. It was decadent yet light, the perfect balance of textures and sweetness.

Bamboo Tripoli has truly mastered the art of delivering an unforgettable dining experience. Whether you’re looking for a special night out or a cozy dinner, this place will exceed your expectations. Highly recommended!

address: 287 Maarad street, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
Duo has always been a great restaurant but their Broumana branch was a little disappointing. 
From a location perspective, it is actually very nicely located on the main road overlooking the heart of the action. The experience is really a nice experience to be on one of the tables at the border of the seating area.
The menu is mostly international with meals for every taste and a daily speciality. They had Escalope on the day I visited and it was not bad. 
The disappointing factor is mainly the prices. Everything is overpriced. A can of Pepsi is for LBP 5,000 (USD 3.5), which is a complete rip off when you actually can buy it for half a dollar
The food is nice but also quite overpriced for the quality and the taste. 
The service does not mark high neither. It was a little slow and took time for them to come and take out order. Also, I was surprised with the cleanliness of some of the utensils they provided us.

DUO brags on being a unique restaurant but my experience with the Broumana branch was quite disappointing. I recommend you stay away from this place until it gets better. You actually have a ton of other choices in the area at a walking distance.

***Please like this review if you find it helpful. It has been drafted to provide you insights on your next trip or visit from the perspective of a fellow consumer and traveler***

address: Rue Daaboul, Beirut Lebanon
review_title: Non Traditional Breakfast with Fresh Cunchy & Stuffed Meat Sandiwches, Friendly Atmosphere, Hidden Gems of Beirut,
review_text:
Background:

It was recommended by one of my friends. His father used to take him there when he was a kid. We went together with other friends and we had our sandwiches feast. It is a ''non – traditional'' Lebanese way of having breakfast.
It is not a man2oushe nor hummus and foul. 
The breakfast here is sandwiches that range from the typical labne and cheese to meat sandwiches such as sujuk (sausages), roast beef, Sawda (the kidney of the chicken) and lsenet (roasted cow tongue).

What to Expect:

It is an old sandwich shop in Beirut:
-The place is small
-seating is basic: stool and aluminum bar
-decoration is minimal. 
-Suitable for a group of friends or a take away for the family.

My own Experience:

The sandwiches I tried were roast beef, sawda and sujuk. The three were crunchy and freshly baked but more importantly stuffed with meat and hence you enjoy the real taste of the sandwich.
I loved how he adds  fresh mint for all the sandiwches

Wait for the owner to call your number and stay aware of it as he will be asking you if you want it spicy or not? with mayonnaise mustard and pickles. The owner is friendly and has his own way in interacting with customers. 

The atmospheres is nice and friendly. Standing with other people waiting for your number and accordingly for the delicious sandwiches!!! A spontaneous chat with the one next to you is unavoidable.

Recommendations:

You have three sizes of sandwiches:
-Half a sandwich (recommended) (2$)
-A medium size sandwich 
-And a large sandwich (4$)
 
 I recommend you to have othe half sandwich option and order two different sandiwches. Afte tthe two i  can assure you you will be satisfyingly FULL!!!
If you want to take it further try the desserts sandwiches which are usually served at home such as jam and halawa

Timing: it is good to go there for breakfast or brunch. He closes in the early afternoon when all the food is sold out.

How to get there: Hidden in the narrow alleys of corniche Al Mazraa. Ask the taxi to drop you next to Paindore and when you reach paindore ask anyone and they will show you. (1 min walking distance)

I regularly go to Punto Alto Restaurant, every time I would like to have a real Italian lunch or dinner.
The restaurant has a charming location.
The staff is very friendly, and serving you professionally and quickly.
Regarding the food, the restaurant serves generously wide range of Italian meals, and you can enjoy as well delicious snacks and desert too.

address: Al Kalamoun, Sea side road, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: Amazing sea view and  tasty Maza dishes. Shishtawooq and grilled Kuba are delicious and the free dessert of ice cream with GazelAlbanet is fabulous. The price is moderate.

address: Al Kalamoun, Sea side road, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text:
Lebanese mezze at its best. You must try the kafta in pomegranate sauce. Delicious...
Clean, friendly service, good food, good prices. What more can u ask for?

address: Al Kalamoun, Sea side road, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text:
If ur in the middle of the city at the mountain or u need a sea view u can find ur branch.
Faysal offer u a great tasty food ,especially oriental food served with excellance 👍🏻i highly recommend

address: Al Kalamoun, Sea side road, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: It's my favorite restaurant in north Lebanon, very clean and delicious food, the staffs are very friendly, highly recommend it

address: Al Kalamoun, Sea side road, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: Very nice place and amazing sea view plus a tasty arabic food always hot and yummy ! Extremely recommend it

address: Ras Maska, Bohssas Lebanon
Old Beirut Road Miramar Resort, Hotel & Spa, Kalamoun, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text:
We visited this place as we invited friends for dinner, and they suggested this restaurant as it was close to their house. Fortunately we came, and ate at this place. Amazing view, authentic environment and super delicious food. Service was fast and waiters were friendly and professional. We finished every bite and plates on that table. The chef passed by to make sure everything was as we expected. They provide complimentary fruits plates and sweets after the meal. 
This restaurant is located in Miramar resort, and had a great beach scene. I am not sure though if you could access the resort from the restaurant, but it looked far walking distance. Highly recommended.

address: Old Beirut Road Miramar Resort, Hotel & Spa, Kalamoun, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: This restaurant at the end of a pier is very nicely located and has seaviews and views on a small yacht dock next to the Miramar hotel. It has a nice and spacious interior with a trendy lay-out. That night we had a group dinner during a conference with a Lebanese mezze. Many classic small dishes were offered and tasted with a friendly ambiance. Quality of the dishes was above average. Reception and service were good.

address: Old Beirut Road Miramar Resort, Hotel & Spa, Kalamoun, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text:
What a gorgeous view! What lovely food! What a relaxing ambiance! What wonderful staff! 
I absolutely loved Bahri Bay which is located in the Miramar Resort in Tripoli. I will definitely visit again, especially at sunset.

address: Old Beirut Road Miramar Resort, Hotel & Spa, Kalamoun, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text:
Perfect place to eat and meet...
Every Sunday they served open international Buffet with amazing food and excellent hospitality 
During the weekdays it's a large Carte and what I love is they served fresh fish & mixed grill in the same time so what you want you can eat

address: Al Manar Tripol, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text:
SOHO premuim street food is the perfect place to eat with kids not being worried from tummy aches or bad quality. 
It's the best in cleanliness, hygiene, service and it's seriously premium as they use fresh and top food quality items!
The taste is unforgettable as we tried all the menu till now since it was opened, we love all the items, the fajitas, burgers especially beef woooow, the special boxes they have satisfy our cravings. As well they have a wonderful ambience which keeps my kids 😊 
I recommend it for every mom cares about her family 😁❤️ 
Special and big Thanks to it's Chef and lovely staff especially Iman 🌹

address: Haykalieh Koura, Tripoli Lebanon
Dam W Zarez, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text:
Dar El Qamar is by far the best restaurant in Tripoli. And can compete for that title on a country level as well.. 
Great Lebanese cuisine, amazing and fast service, very spacious and continuously innovative and introducing new items to the menu.. can't recommend it enough.

address: Dam W Zarez, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: The staff is so welcoming and caring, the food was perfect and delicious. One of the best lebanese restaurants. I would definitely recommend it, and im definitely coming back!😍

address: Dam W Zarez, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: veryy generous amount of foodd with high quality ingredients!! The staff are very good and u feel like u're sitting in ur home

All waiters are very very friendly’s !!!!

address: Dam W Zarez, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text:
If you’re planing to have Lebanese food at elite level, then head to this place, perfect food, service and so delicious sweets.

Thanks to all the crew

Food was very tasty. 
Prices affordable with good portions.

Staff very friendly & welcoming

address: Achir Daye Street, Tripoli Lebanon

Best time we had spent

address: Achir Daye Street, Tripoli Lebanon

*Cheddar fried balls.

address: Beirut Hwy, in front Quality Inn, Beside Ferri Gallery Mina, Tripoli Lebanon

address: Beirut Hwy, in front Quality Inn, Beside Ferri Gallery Mina, Tripoli Lebanon
review_title: Seeking for something special in north lebanon
review_text:
Hospitality wise is excellent from chefs, taste (quality of food), staff, ambience and presentation.
The board of directors is well composed and qualified to accomplish their jobs properly. 
It is recommended to visit the place with family and friends. 
Cheap according to the taste and quality of food. 
Action speaks louder than words, its the best decision you can take.

address: Achir Daye Street, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: Excellent mezza, good prices, good sheesha, generous dishes great service but doesn't have a view. Good place for family

address: Achir Daye Street, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text:

Service is professional and polite
Life music is available with a nice oud player / singer 
Very recommend for all tripoli visitors

address: Achir Daye Street, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: El Hajj Ali is a restaurant where you can sit and eat in Tripoli away from the crowd and noise.Its location is in Tripoli but you dont have to enter inside Dam w Farz(street where all restaurants and caffes are located).Its building is designed as a spanish building.The food is delicious and clean.The waiters are friendly.

address: Achir El Daya Street Dam West Farez, Tripoli Lebanon
review_title: One of the best cocktail places in tripoli
review_text:
Quite and very nice ambiance , excellent quality of fresh and other fruits, friendly and responsive staff, wide variety of fruits all over the seasons . moderate prices.
But what i like most about this place , is that i can order my customized cocktail whenever i like .
The icecream is also yummy and various .And fresh Juices are always available.

address: Achir El Daya Street Dam West Farez, Tripoli Lebanon
review_title: Best ice cream and cocktail in town
review_text:
When you visit tripoli you should pass by papaya the most delicious cocktail and ice cream in town 
You can find so many choices in ice cream like mango,  strawberry, lemon, blackberry, coconut, dry fruit, chocolate, Oreo, vanilla and so many ... ♥️😍😍😍🍧🍧🍹🍹🍹

address: Achir El Daya Street Dam West Farez, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: I went there twice, and ate Italian ice cream. The flavors are diverse and the taste very good and fresh. The prices are acceptable too and the inner hall is fine for sitting and enjoying my dessert.

address: Achir El Daya Street Dam West Farez, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text:
Clean and fresh ingredients, staff is polite, 
My advise is to stay away from canned fruits, 
Better to use a smaller variety of fresh fruits than a larger one with some canned fruits

address: Achir El Daya Street Dam West Farez, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: This is a neat restaurant, clean and friendly, most of its customers are polite and elegant, price is very good for the high quality provided, Papaya is good choice for families with children too, highly recommended.

address: Achier El Daya, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text:
Amazing vibe and service - beautiful cafe and a great manager ! 
Beautiful food and very tasty! 

Thank you  for a lovely evening 

Hayfa

address: Achier El Daya, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text:
The food was amazing and they were all fresh ingredients. For a person who had
a long day i recommend you to come to Rawand. Service was good and the food had a lot of variety. But i recommend Rawand to upgrade their steak since it wasn’t the best but other than that. Amazing experience 👍

address: Achier El Daya, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: The place is perfect for gatherings. The customer service is perfect and the food is super delicious and high quality. I would recommend only adding some traditional oldies Arabic songs or music morning time.

address: Achier El Daya, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: The place is nice, the food was surprisingly amazing, all fresh ingredients. The waiters are super professional, not an argileh fan but the place is very well ventilated.

address: Achier El Daya, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: I ordered chicken parmigiana without cheese, and baked potatoes instead of pasta. the first time they got the chicken with cheese, the second time they got the chicken without the tomato sauce, and pasta instead of potatoes. they apologized but they didn’t actually do anything about it. I heard the waitress talk with someone (not sure if he’s the manager or the supervisor) but still he just looked at me without doing anything, not even apologizing. Very bad experience!!!

address: 32nd Street, Tripoli 00902 Lebanon
review_text:
My favourite pizzeria in North Lebanon
Fresh ingredients 
Authentic recipes 
Wood oven 
Delicious salads
You can't go wrong with any of the dishes
Highly recommended

address: 32nd Street, Tripoli 00902 Lebanon
review_text: Cozy place. I like the asparagus lemon pasta. Its great with a strong flavor. Melanzan pizza is my favorite (i love egglants). To try for sure

address: 32nd Street, Tripoli 00902 Lebanon
review_text: The pizza is good, best in Tripoli, sit indoor so you can get wine & beer, check the board for daily specials, good place to feed a family.

address: 32nd Street, Tripoli 00902 Lebanon
review_text: This pizzeria is an authentic Italian place that serves firewood baked pizzas. The taste is really authentic because the combination of crust and sauce is one of a kind in this region where all restaurants serve the same dishes

address: 32nd Street, Tripoli 00902 Lebanon
review_title: The Pizza can never taste better
review_text:
Who said that good pizza ate only available on italy? Una pizza is the place where you can enjoy the best pizza and in a wide variety , great salads and tasty deserts
Make sure to save some space for the chocolate pizza at the end

address: Dam el Farez Street 32, Tripoli, Lebanon, Tripoli Lebanon
