review_title: Not a good place to hang out for coffee.
review_text: I ordered American coffee with milk on the side. I had to wait  forever to get my coffee and when it arrived it was cold and the milk is not included. I had to wait another 15 to 20 minutes to get the milk and when it arrived, it was too cold to drink it.

address: Riad El Solh St Facing Mina central and Max Department Store, Mina, Tripoli Lebanon
review_title: Nice, friendly atmosphere and staff. Great location. Most importantly, there is no indoor smoking allowed.
review_text: Nice, friendly atmosphere and staff. Great location. Most importantly, there is no indoor smoking allowed.

address: Riad El Solh St Facing Mina central and Max Department Store, Mina, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: I did not know this place before and was just walking around for sightseeing. When i saw this cafe i thought: i can rest here and sip my coffee. I ordered an espresso and it was tasteful. I really liked the aroma and enjoyed my espresso. I didn't taste the other coffee but it seems that they are doing great in coffee roasters.

address: Mina, El Mina, Tripoli Lebanon
review_title: Nice place to be when in Tripoli-Mina
review_text:
Mediterranean sea food place; Nice company, stories from a multi lingual Sea Captain.
Ask for the Mezze variety, insist if they have raw fish.
I recommend fried "Mallifa" Fish if available or any Grilled fish.

address: Mina, El Mina, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: Minos is a hidden jewel not far from the coast. it's a very nice place to enjoy some wine with seafood. the only annoying part is when other customers start drunk singing, it can be very loud and unpleasant because the setup is very small.

address: Mina, El Mina, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: Very good mezze and grilled fish in Mina, worth the visit. Small detail: the fish menu is rather small, but after the mezze with different raw fish options, you hardly want to eat more...

address: Mina, El Mina, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: Do not be afraid by the deco which is far from the bling bling Beyrouth style. The menu is only 25000 and for this you ll have 5 very good starters then a full dorade in the oven . The boss is a former captain and is very nice . The wine is at the same price than in the shop . Go there , a very nice time to spend .

address: Mina, El Mina, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: This is a hidden gem once you discover you'll become a regular there! The place is tucked away between EL Mina buildings on Groupie roundabout so you will need to ask around if this is your first time. Trust me, it's worth it! For around 30 USD you will get a set menu of all kinds of mezza dishes plus the catch of the day. The place does not have shisha but they will accommodate your request if you would like one. If you are a large group of 20, you can request that the whole place be booked for you only. You can purchase alcohol and play whatever music you would like. The place also has a TV and AC's for when it's too cold/hot.

address: Ashraf Kabbara Street, Tripoli Lebanon
Tripoli Dam W Farez Main Road, Tripoli 0096 Lebanon
review_text: Cosy place, not recommend for family with young children, sheesha smoking restaurant,  average food, good service, mid range prices. If you are a  sheesha smoker, it is good to visit. It serves Food,  juices, coffee, sheesha all day.

address: Tall Street, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: I visited Tripoli branch with family , wasn’t like before  specially the Barazi2 ZERO, I asked the lady on the cashier if the Barazi2 have pistachio she confirmed, I opened the first box No pistachio so I showed the cashier lady she point on her manage and said He’s responsible, so I showed the manager that there is no pistachio by flipping the small pcs one by one, he didn’t act and I end up putting the 2 box I. The garbage

address: Tall Street, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text:
Took a prepared  baklava to visit family turns out to be old!  Will give it to the chickens on my farm!  

How many days do you leave items out to sell and claim to be fresh!  I would avoid this place I should have went to cremino!

address: Tall Street, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: Located at Tripoli Lebanon. Visit this place for the first time for lunch. The food was just ok but the Lebanese sweets were tasty as usual. They service was good too. We paid around 18eur per person. To try the sweets only.

address: Tall Street, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: I’m in Australia and I ordered baklava from #Rafaat Hallab  it just took a 5 days to get it and the taste was so good and fresh I loved it 😍

address: Abdul Hamid Karami Square, Tripoli 1300 Lebanon
review_text: Arabic sweets the best also knefeh my best one u can order anytime for every event its very fancy desserts

address: Abdul Hamid Karami Square, Tripoli 1300 Lebanon
review_title: Delicious sweets, cake and lahm b ajeen
review_text: on the go pick up, this place is just near the city center, and if you're in a hurry to go from Tripoli heading south, this place is perfect to try some lebanese sweets or "lahm b ajeen" , take away or sitting for a while to eat then go.

address: Abdul Hamid Karami Square, Tripoli 1300 Lebanon
review_text: Not a maven of Middle Eastern desserts.  Being used to the cloyingly sweet version of Greek baklava, I was pleasantly surprised with the texture and less sweet version of various baklava's from Al Amina-tom sweet.  I'm sure that persons of Middle Eastern dissent have a far more discerning palate for various Palestinian, Egyptian and Lebanese confections, but I was very pleased with the assortment of sweets that I purchased.

address: Al Koura Square Mohammad El Husseini Street, Tripoli 1300 Lebanon
review_title: olive half eaten found in foul plate
review_text: i came from canada straight to the restaurant missing authentic lebanese foul and hommus the surprise was tha i found olive half eaten in the foul plate what that mean ? the food reming from customer add to the new customer plate?? i give zero star

address: Al Koura Square Mohammad El Husseini Street, Tripoli 1300 Lebanon
review_title: Typical restaurant in the middle of the Tripoli souk
review_text: Typical restaurant in the middle of the Tripoli souk, the most vibrant souk in Lebanon! wonderfully decorated, with an exhibition of clocks, telephones and old cameras. Very varied and excellent traditional Lebanese food, with dishes to share. The service is very friendly, as in most Lebanese restaurants.

address: Al Koura Square Mohammad El Husseini Street, Tripoli 1300 Lebanon
review_text: Might just be the best foul I ever had in Trablous, and thats saying a lot since foul is something they know how to make. Cool interior and efficient waiters.

address: Al Koura Square Mohammad El Husseini Street, Tripoli 1300 Lebanon
review_text: So disgusting, I felt like the dish only looked good on the outside and on the inside tasted like people's droppings, I was very disappointed

address: Al Koura Square Mohammad El Husseini Street, Tripoli 1300 Lebanon
review_text:
Experience an authentic breakfast with an original taste before you start your day off in the city!
best ambiance & service providd

review_text:
The access is easy. The owner Mr Bassam receive you always with smile and happy to serve you. The personnel are kind. The place is very clean and the food is really tasty. If you come to Tripoli don’t miss the place and eat the best mankoushen in Tripoli

address: Riad El Solh Str., Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: I had a very bad experience with their online orders, a breakfast of knefe turned into a launch of knefe with 2 hrs delay, some products were missing, their website is not precise. Even though it is tasty but it is not professional. I don't recommend

address: Riad El Solh Str., Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: Had a lovely stop at Abdel Rahman Hallab in Batroun! The ice cream was absolutely delicious, the place is charming and well-kept, and the whole team was welcoming and professional. A special shout-out to Lea Farah, our waitress, she was so attentive, kind, and genuinely supportive. Her warm smile and thoughtful service made the experience even more enjoyable. Highly recommend visiting!

address: Riad El Solh Str., Tripoli Lebanon
review_text:
Shipping to the US.
We paid more than $200 for your stupid variety box , you charged $35 to ship , and we found out that the sweet selection in it are more than a month old …. This  business  is an insult to any honorable Lebanese business , it’s just a Dumpster.

address: Riad El Solh Str., Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: Verry delicious but verrrry bad in changing the price in same day after 3 hours, they increase the price, this is unfair i prefer to change the manager

address: Riad El Solh Str., Tripoli Lebanon
review_title: Excellent products. Service and Front Staff Needs Training (And Better Pay?)
review_text: Hallab 1881, known to everyone as Kasr el Helou, has by far the best quality sweets in Lebanon. No other establishment comes close. However, the front staff in their main  Tripoli branch desperately needs training in customer service. Their front servers need to THOROUGHLY clean their tables AND chairs. Their cashiers need to be better responsive to customers' requests. Both the front staff and cashiers seem aloof and uncaring. A smiley, friendly face is a dime a dozen over there.

address: El Mina Street, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: Amzzaing food and very good quality really need to visit so many different types of food and so many places you can go to the restaurant especially the one in the mountains

address: El Mina Street, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: I visited the restaurant couple of times and everytime I am really amazed with the quality of food.  Indeed they are very generous in serving their customers.

address: El Mina Street, Tripoli Lebanon
review_title: Family, business, or take away you are in the right place
review_text:
Traditional and clean Lebanese food at its best 
Also great choice for delivery
excellent customer service

address: El Mina Street, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: Lebanese "mezze" at its best. Great kebab and roasted meat (the Lebanese way). Prices are very reasonable. Polite staff, Valet Parking available. Just note that alcoholic drinks might not be available in most restaurants of Tripoli ..

address: El Mina Street, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text:
Pro:
- very spacious
- reasonably priced meals
- a cool breeze during summer
- service is good
- friendly staff

Cons:
- Parking is difficult so get a small car.

address: Rasmasqua Tripoli Highway, Tripoli 0096 Lebanon
review_text: It is very good restaurant the food is excellent, the view is out of this world, all the ocean is in front of you from up a hill, the service is very good, there is access for wheelchairs, it is a must visit if you come to Lebanon. The food price is very reasonable.

address: Al moutran street., Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: A great place to visit, excellent food vegan, friendly staff,very generous presentation and cheap,i highly recommend this place.

address: Al moutran street., Tripoli Lebanon
review_title: Still eating typically popular Lebanese food
review_text: Very clean restaurant where you have have the typical popular Lebanese food: hommos, Foul, fateh. If you visit Tripoli don’t miss to pass by this restaurant.

address: Al moutran street., Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: Al Dannoun is famous for its Foul Mudammas and Hummus with the hot bread and free green mint and pickles with olive. It is a must when you visit Tripoly. Price is low.

address: Al moutran street., Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: Traditional restaurant, tourist point, must visit when going to Tripoli city. Solo or family visit. Average service . Very clean.

address: Al moutran street., Tripoli Lebanon
review_text:
Being from Tripoli, Lebanon I grew up on the taste of Dannoun; not only for breakfast but anytime during the day whenever your crave that amazing taste of Humus or Fatteh and Foul. What amazes me is that after leaving the country for like 15 years, it still have the same exquisite taste and original flavor! This is a family business well kept.
Well done and keep it up.

address: Main Street Dam W Farz, Tripoli 3116 Lebanon
review_text: A nice place where one can enjoy goof food at great prices , prompt and punctual service, optimal hygiene and with the help of a very dedicated, friendly and professional staff.

address: Main Street Dam W Farz, Tripoli 3116 Lebanon
review_text: It has a good variety of healthy food. The waitresses and waiters are very friendly helpful and attentive.

address: Main Street Dam W Farz, Tripoli 3116 Lebanon
review_text: This place is one of the worse restaurants I have ever visited. We went to this place after some friends recommended it as the best place to have a breakfast in Tripoli! We drove like 30 minutes to get to this restaurant. Service was super super slow, it took 45 minutes to bring two omelette!!! We were the only people in the restaurant though. The waiter was not friendly and all. The power went off for the entire time we were there, and the manager didn’t hurt herself to even apologize for that inconvenience. To make it worst, there was re-construction for the place, so we had to sit eat in the heat, and hear all the wonderful nose coming from outside. I do not recommend this place.

address: Azmi Street, Tripoli Lebanon
El Mina, Groopy Roundabout, Next to Shoppies Supermarket, Tripoli Lebanon
review_title: Lunch at Sayad's Sandwishes in Tripoli
review_text: Walking into Sayad's Sandwishes, a shabby little kiosk, you might not expect to find some of the freshest fish sandwishes in Tripoli. Made to order, people stand in line to enjoy their tasty fried fish, spicy fish, shrimp, octopus and calamari sandwishes.

address: El Mina, Groopy Roundabout, Next to Shoppies Supermarket, Tripoli Lebanon
review_title: the tinny shop with unforgettable taste
review_text: all kind of sea food sandwiches, only the fresh ingredients and the exceptional cooking technique... Hungry for a quick sea food? do not hesitate

address: El Mina, Groopy Roundabout, Next to Shoppies Supermarket, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: Although it has one of the best seafood sandwishes in tripoli, it is really expensive comparing to other restaurants in the city. Anyway, if you wish to have a great taste and fresh ingredients, it still is the best shop

address: El Mina, Groopy Roundabout, Next to Shoppies Supermarket, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: Al Sayad is the best ever seafood sandwiches in Lebanon. Fresh shrimps, fish, calamari & octopus prepared with the chef sauce & displayed in front of you on a daily basis. Go there to Mina - mar Elias street / groupi roundabout, ask for the sayad - Ayoub Zaini. You won't regret it.

address: Minot, Tripoli Lebanon
Yaacoub Labban Street Facing Jesuit's School, Mina 1301 Lebanon
review_title: The best authentic fish restaurant in town
review_text: The best fish restaurant in town. Family business, very nice team, great food and quick service. Bring your alcohol and enjoy a great fresh meal. They introduced non-sea food dishes also.  Price is great. Highly recommended.

address: Labban Street Across Beit El Nessim Tripoli, Mina Lebanon
Jeme'ei Omar, Mina Lebanon
review_title: A wonderful little gem of a restaurant
review_text:
Our food wasn't the best in our all inclusive hotel in Hurghada and we found this little gem of a place round the corner.
Owner and staff were lovely and friendly and made us feel very welcome.
Restaurant was clean and very tidy.
The food was so delicious I had prawns, calamari dishes galore with all the trimmings.
On our last couple of nights they made our butterfly prawns nice and spicey for us nothing was too much for them
The fruit juices were made fresh and were perfect with the meals.
Thank you team you were all wonderful and made our holiday extra special.
Many thanks
Joanne Wright

address: Jeme'ei Omar, Mina Lebanon
review_text: Staff were lovely lobster was so tender and juicy restaurant was very clean and we got a seafood feast for just £14! If you in hurghada this is a must go to

address: Jeme'ei Omar, Mina Lebanon
review_title: Fresh fish is the driver for this place
review_text:
The main course is an easy choice : the waiter brings you to the fresh fish market two steps down the road, you select the fish you want and you get it back grilled or fried back at your table. It cannot be a shorter way from sea to teeth, and price is very competitive. 
Recommended by a local, I approve this place too.

address: Corniche El Mina El Istiklal Street, Tripoli Lebanon
Al-Mina, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text:
The place was recommended by a local and we were not disappointed! 
The view was nice and the food was super delicious, fresh and authentic.
The fish and sauces are very well done, and the price much better than similar fish restaurants closer to Beirut!

address: 287 Maarad street, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
Duo has always been a great restaurant but their Broumana branch was a little disappointing. 
From a location perspective, it is actually very nicely located on the main road overlooking the heart of the action. The experience is really a nice experience to be on one of the tables at the border of the seating area.
The menu is mostly international with meals for every taste and a daily speciality. They had Escalope on the day I visited and it was not bad. 
The disappointing factor is mainly the prices. Everything is overpriced. A can of Pepsi is for LBP 5,000 (USD 3.5), which is a complete rip off when you actually can buy it for half a dollar
The food is nice but also quite overpriced for the quality and the taste. 
The service does not mark high neither. It was a little slow and took time for them to come and take out order. Also, I was surprised with the cleanliness of some of the utensils they provided us.

DUO brags on being a unique restaurant but my experience with the Broumana branch was quite disappointing. I recommend you stay away from this place until it gets better. You actually have a ton of other choices in the area at a walking distance.

***Please like this review if you find it helpful. It has been drafted to provide you insights on your next trip or visit from the perspective of a fellow consumer and traveler***

address: Rue Daaboul, Beirut Lebanon
review_title: Non Traditional Breakfast with Fresh Cunchy & Stuffed Meat Sandiwches, Friendly Atmosphere, Hidden Gems of Beirut,
review_text:
Background:

It was recommended by one of my friends. His father used to take him there when he was a kid. We went together with other friends and we had our sandwiches feast. It is a ''non – traditional'' Lebanese way of having breakfast.
It is not a man2oushe nor hummus and foul. 
The breakfast here is sandwiches that range from the typical labne and cheese to meat sandwiches such as sujuk (sausages), roast beef, Sawda (the kidney of the chicken) and lsenet (roasted cow tongue).

What to Expect:

It is an old sandwich shop in Beirut:
-The place is small
-seating is basic: stool and aluminum bar
-decoration is minimal. 
-Suitable for a group of friends or a take away for the family.

My own Experience:

The sandwiches I tried were roast beef, sawda and sujuk. The three were crunchy and freshly baked but more importantly stuffed with meat and hence you enjoy the real taste of the sandwich.
I loved how he adds  fresh mint for all the sandiwches

Wait for the owner to call your number and stay aware of it as he will be asking you if you want it spicy or not? with mayonnaise mustard and pickles. The owner is friendly and has his own way in interacting with customers. 

The atmospheres is nice and friendly. Standing with other people waiting for your number and accordingly for the delicious sandwiches!!! A spontaneous chat with the one next to you is unavoidable.

Recommendations:

You have three sizes of sandwiches:
-Half a sandwich (recommended) (2$)
-A medium size sandwich 
-And a large sandwich (4$)
 
 I recommend you to have othe half sandwich option and order two different sandiwches. Afte tthe two i  can assure you you will be satisfyingly FULL!!!
If you want to take it further try the desserts sandwiches which are usually served at home such as jam and halawa

Timing: it is good to go there for breakfast or brunch. He closes in the early afternoon when all the food is sold out.

How to get there: Hidden in the narrow alleys of corniche Al Mazraa. Ask the taxi to drop you next to Paindore and when you reach paindore ask anyone and they will show you. (1 min walking distance)

Too bad smoking was not allowed, the place provides an excellent mood for Cigars.

address: Place Du Musee, Beirut Lebanon
review_title: Magical Candy Store from a Different Era
review_text:
Have you ever wanted to go back in a time machine to a more innocent time, Before Things Got So Bad? In Lebanon, that means before the currency devaluation, before the economic free-fall, before the political sclerosis and corruption, before the port explosion, before Covid, and before the Civil War.  

Le Marzipan, located in a little street right across from the National Museum, is just such a magical spot.  Founded in 1964, its owners have literally never left the building. When the place was shot up during the 1980's civil war, they insisted on rebuilding it EXACTLY as it was. The traditional candy cooker in the back is still used every day, and their traditional chewy liquorice and caramels are still amazing. The woman, who speaks French and English, will welcome you with a timeless Middle Eastern hospitality.  The cost for a big bag of candy –pennies to foreign tourists– will help keep a precious memory of Old Time Beirut alive.  YOU MUST SEE THIS PLACE! It's only a few meters from either the National museum or the MIM minerals museum.  Fantastic!

review_title: Great fish - Great and friendly service
review_text:
We spied this tiny restaurant when walking back to our hotel at around 4 in the afternoon and it was full of people with a chalk sign outside that declared Fruit de mere. We decided to go back later in the evening with our 5 year old daughter. We were a little trepidatious at first as it was empty but we decided to give it a go. 
We sat down and were asked if we would like to pick our fish and were led next door to an equally tiny fish shop where an array of fish were on-ice and in small displays around the wall. We chose a recommended fish of 1.2 Kilos and returned to sit down.
The very friendly, I am guessing the owner, greeted us and guessed we would not understand the menu in Arabic so he suggested a traditional fettoush salad, french fries and offered us a variety of other appetizers from which we chose grilled calamari.
The salad was fresh and dressed with a little grenadine, wonderful fries and calamari.
When the fish arrived it was fillet at the table, I love fish and this was truly excellent.
A bottle of excellent local rose wine plus water and orange juice for the kid and the whole thing with tip was $94 plus tip.
I liked it so much I added the place to TA!

review_text:
The restaurant is a bit hidden under an appartment block at the end of Hamra street, but well worth going. It is a very colourful place that is open 24/7, and serves vegan food exclusively. I’m an omnivore, but the taste of everything that was served was divine and at a great price.

They serve sandwiches, soups, mezze, pizza and pasta, and I was spoiled for choice. I took the lentil soup and the server recommended Intake the vegan cheese sticks with it. The sauces that were served with the cheese complimented them perfectly, especially the cashew-based sauce was finger-licking good. The soup was comforting and served with a fattoush salad. I also took one of the cheesecakes as dessert. Each dish cost around 140,000, which is very reasonable for the quality served

I highly recommend this place if you want some quality lebanese vegan food (or even if you’re an omnivore like me, because I didn’t miss anything on my plate).

review_text:
Capitale Café is honestly my favorite place and I always find myself going back. The atmosphere is warm, stylish, and perfect whether you’re meeting friends or just enjoying some quiet time. The food is consistently delicious; from their savory items to their desserts, everything tastes fresh and well prepared. What I love the most is the presentation; every plate looks beautiful and carefully done, which makes the whole experience even better.
The team is always helpful, welcoming, and attentive without being overwhelming. You really feel taken care of. During Ramadan, they also offer a special Iftar formula for those who love to break their fast in a cozy yet elegant setting, with a great selection and generous portions.
For me, Capitale isn’t just a café; it’s a place I keep coming back to. Highly recommended.

review_title: Close to being excellent...a few tweaks.
review_text:
As a bar restaurant, stylistically its laid out and furnished in a dated cabaret style.
The menu is, as you might expect given the concept, limited. However, it has enough dishes and these are interesting.
Its an excellent wine list, again limited and well priced…with the exception of sole Lebanese inclusion. This raised my eyebrows. I’ve been to that vineyard and know its wines.
I ordered the fish carpaccio and beef filet. Unfortunately, both arrived together, the weak excuse being they were sharing dishes. No, particularly when each person ordered separately. That said, hands were put up and the main course was cooked again in a timely manner.
The fish was lovely. Meaty, with a nice play between the mango and red chilli. I detected aniseed, specifically Pernod. Apparently its not a component so it might have been a welcome cross contamination. I’d encourage the chef to play with it. Just a little really adds to the dish.
With a rich cognac pepper sauce and perfectly seasoned fresh new potatoes, my sadly overcooked filet arrived. Overcooked meat is one sin, but I must question the quality of the meat. Even overcooked it should cut like butter. This cut like playdough, a stringy smear following my repeated efforts to cut off pieces.
It’s a real shame because they have a great floor team and the meat element aside, have got something special here.
At nearly US$40 for a filet steak in Beirut, the quality of the meat should not be what is holding them back.
Will I be back? I don’t know. I’ll keep an eye on future reviews and see what is being said.

address: Rue Khaled Chehab, Beirut Lebanon

review_text:
As soon as we stepped inside, the atmosphere was welcoming, and beautifully designed and set the tone to a memorable meal.
The perfect balance between cozy and elegant. The lighting, music, and decor all contributed to a relaxed, enjoyable environment that made us feel right at home.

The food was equally impressive. Every dish was fresh, flavorful, and perfectly cooked. You can really taste the care and quality in each bite. The quinoa and  shrimp was a standout, rich, and beautifully presented and the hummos with herbs was also very delicious.
In the other side, it was my first time tasting the fattet fawaregh, and I was honestly blown away. From the very first bite, the flavors were rich, perfectly balanced, and unexpectedly satisfying. The texture, the seasoning, the aroma, everything came together in the best way. I didn’t expect to love it this much, but it quickly became a new favorite. It was one of those meals you remember long after the last bite.

The staff were friendly and attentive without being intrusive. Overall, a fantastic dining experience. Highly recommended for anyone who appreciates great food in a great setting!

address: Monot Street, Beirut 0000 Lebanon
review_title: One of the last still standing nightclubs in Beirut
review_text:
Nestled in one of the smallest streets in Ashrafyeh, Almodovar quickly became one of the busiest pub/night clubs in Beirut. And there’s a reason for this. 
To start with the food is quite interesting with modern fusion Mexican menus and is better than one would expect in a night club. Literally everything is presented with exotic dips that will make your taste buds dance throughout the meal. Also, they have one of the best drinks menus for such a venue. You can order almost any kind of drink or cocktail and you will surely get it. 
However this NOT what makes Almodovar really interesting. The secret is at post 10pm this very particular place transforms into a dancing floor where one can dance to one hearts delight. The music selection is exquisite and on point and the crowds will definitely go wild. THIS is Almodovar’s little secret and it’s a good one. 
Still, a few little details can make or break the mood. For one, some high tables are too small and uncomfortable especially if it’s a group of 3+ pax. Also, on some special nights it is truly overcrowded. Claustrophobic people should avoid. 
Still it is a must do experience. Word of the wise make sure you reserve a good table. My personal recommendation is to try it on a Thursday or Sunday. Both are exceptional nights there. Enjoy!

2- However the food was very disappointing for the value we paid $75 per person. The food was basically only appetizers!! And we got some fruit, chocolate bar, meghli, and chocolate rolls and rice pudding at the end!! 

3-All lebanese mezzes usually come with a main course either bbq (meat/fish) i was very disappointed will not come back again!! I felt we were ripped off!!! 

3- Seems they are focusing more on the performances going on than the food itself! 

4- The restaurant is kind of small and not sure if they have a ventilation system because the restaurant was filled with white smoke from hooka and cigs. 

I wouldn’t recommend this place to anyone. All restaurants in lebanon have performances with better fixed menus! Which includes a main course!!

address: Monot Street, Beirut Lebanon
review_title: If you are looking for a restaurant don’t go there
review_text: The lady taking the reservation was not really courteous, she did offer only an indoor table but forgot to mention that it is an all smoking space. The food was very slow to arrive, the main dishes arrived wrong and before the appetizers. They never corrected the wrong dish and had to leave before my wife had had dinner.

address: Monot Street, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
It is never my choice to go to Pacifico, but I only go if a friend insists like crazy.
9 times out of 10, you will get bad service, and I’m an extremely patient person, but even the Pope would get frustrated.
You have to wait and go out of your way to catch a server’s attention and when you do, the service is half-assed: you either get your food without cutlery, or your wine without the glasses etc… 
And the music is nightclub level loud!! Why? 
And don’t get me started on how close the tables are. I’m a small person but I’m always uncomfortably close to either a wall or the person at the next table.
Also, to the owners, please explain to the front of the house that being rude to customers is not “cool”, it’s just bad customer service

address: Monot Street, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: I will not recommend this place to anyone! Food was ok not fabulous! But the service was terrible! They obliged us fixed timing from 6 till 8 and they told us to leave at 8 ! We were a group of 12 ! And none of us had this bad experience! Service bad ! Reservation team bad ! Receiptionest bad!

address: Monot Street, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: Amazing place, friendly staff, professional management and yummy food. One of the oldest bars in Lebanon. A must try!

address: Monot Street, Beirut Lebanon
review_title: Cool joint, avoid going as large group
review_text: This place is cool, always packed and has a good vibe. Unfortunately we went as a large group and they put us on a fixed menu with overpriced food. The menu looked good, but we were stuck with the fajitas, and expensive ones at that. Service was good, and I recommend making a reservation if you're thinking about going. I would return, but only with 4 or Les people, I don't recommend this place for larger groups.

address: Monot St, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: Fine dining in an authentic Lebanese house elevates the experience to a whole new level, with a very friendly and helpful staff. In terms of food, I preferred the entree to the main courses. We began with a salade de sucrine croquante with avocados, followed by warm starters of frogs (cuisses de grenouilles) and wild mushrooms, all of which were delicious. While I ordered Filet de boeuf angus and Filet de porc caramelisé as main courses, they were not to my liking. The most delicious pain perdu rounded out the meal. They had run out of the supreme au chocolat, which was a must-have.

address: Monot St, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: Lovely setting and nice ambiance. service is good with attention to details. Food is somewhat overpriced, some dishes are better than others. Generous portion of mussles, excellent baba au rhum.

address: Monot St, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: This is an expensive restaurant, but the food and service are exceptional. I have eaten here for business, and this is a great place to take clients. Nothing disappoints. My last meal was superb. A party of 12, we left feeling well-satisfied.

address: Monot St, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: Stove has a classy set up , and serves delicious fine dining cuisine. The continuous attention carried out by the owner is remarkable, adding taste  and touch to the dishes and the place itself.  Good Job Toni . keep it up.

address: Monot St, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
A nice atmosphere, lovely interior and good service 
The wine and food was good and tasty 
Lots of choices 
Salad was tasty 
My fish was very well prepared 
And deserts was good
We all liked it a lot 
It is also very responsible 
I recommend this nice restaurant 
U will enjoy it

address: Huvelin Monot, next to USJ Huvelin, Beirut Lebanon
Roucheid El Dahdah, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
We were a group of friends to watch the final WC game. The place was super busy. male toilets were  stinky. Games food selection is mediocre with at lot of fried foods and service is average. 
Overpriced for what we had. It’s 30$ per person for nothing.

address: Roucheid El Dahdah, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
We we highly impressed by this cozy restaurant in the heart of Beirut.
We enjoyed delicious shawarma, mixed grill, houmous, tea and Lebanese desert. 
The staff was very friendly and helpful.
The restaurant has exceptionally warm atmosphere and superb location.
Definitely, a must visit place in the Middle East.

address: Roucheid El Dahdah, Beirut Lebanon
review_title: Hmm looked lovely but contained a few surprises
review_text: The Shababik Garden has a wonderful outside seating area and is welcoming. Groups of women sat around smoking sheesha pipes and the atmosphere was warm. We were 5 and ordered the set meal for US$23 this came with an amazing array of starters including pesto hummus, tabouleh, excellent salads, sausages and everything you expect. The main courses consisted of cooked meats - all of which were good. So the food was excellent but we ordered some beer and wine so between the 5 of us we had two bottles of local wine and 5 beers this doubled the cost of the bill. The bill for food was fine but the costs of the alcohol seemed excessive...so go for food but avoid the wine choices...a pit really..

address: 38 Rue Monot, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: We had a pleasant food experience accompanied by good service and attention to details. Dishes were very well served, some better than others. I would recommend the seabass and the truffle pizzetta. The place attracts an elite crowd, though you don't see fancy dressup, which makes it quite a comfortable place. Good experience overall, to be repeated.

address: 38 Rue Monot, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
We ended up in this restaurant without aiming at it really. I am not sure it has an indoor seating as most service and people were outdoor, on a side walk. Nicely designed so you do not really feel the "side Walk" effect.
Service was great, very professional with a lot of attention to the patrons. Very friendly too.
Prices are on a high however and we opted for cocktails rather than wine because of the prices. Same bottle the night before somewhere else at $45.00 was at $75.00!!!
Still cocktails were not cheap, but this is the way in Beirut for any restaurant that wants itself fancy and trendy.
I had a fish that was nicely and perfectly cooked, very fresh. My wife had a salad and she liked it too.
Recommendable but a bit expensive

address: 38 Rue Monot, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: Stem is a restaurant where the beautiful people come to be seen and to see...with indoor and outdoor eating spaces it has a contemporary chic ambience. The service is well mannered and understated but excellent.  The food is international and we were five and ordered for starters the excellent spicy tuna ( amazing), chilled asparagus (hmm), grouper carpaccio ( as good as it looked) and gazpacho...we followed this with excellent seconds of veal milanese ( wow), squid ink linguine( looked and tasted excellent), the well cooked steak and salmon. The food was all perfectly done and tasted divine ...wine was good. This is not a cheap restaurant but it was worth the visit....excellent

address: 38 Rue Monot, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: Great variety of food, tasty nice trendy place ,vegetarian dishes , great service welcoming staff, yummy home made sorbets and deserts

address: 38 Rue Monot, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: Though you feel like having dinner on the pavement, but this is the trend nowadays. Friendly and professional waiter. Good selection of starters but the beef carpaccio was little and tasteless. The highlight was the shrimps with coconut milk and jasmine rice. Deserts are served automatically , kenafa with a twist was delicious. The place was full, better to book in advance.

address: Choucri Ghanem Street, Ain Mreisse, Beirut District, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: The restaurant is located direct at the sea with a very nice ambience. Get your Shisha an some delicious food and relax. You will love it.

address: Choucri Ghanem Street, Ain Mreisse, Beirut District, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: A very good restaurant. The food is absolutely delicious, delightful and fresh , the staff is highly professional and friendly and the ambience is nice as well.  The service is prompt and punctual and the hygiene is optimal too.

If your kitchen closes at three just mention in Google and if I arrive at 245 then you are supposed to welcome me and no need to be rude. The waiter controls the weak owner. For ducks sake my parents used to go there long time ago. Your esteemed rest states clearly on Google that you close 3.30.

address: Chaccour Trad Street Tabaris, Beirut Lebanon

review_text: This is an old renowned restaurant in Beirut and it has recently been refurbished. The food is delicious and of excellent quality. The menu is varied and their meat exceptional. The restaurant is also ideal for a romantic dinner.

address: Chaccour Trad Street Tabaris, Beirut Lebanon

address: Abou Rizk Building, Abdel Wahab El Englizi Street Ashrafieh, Beirut Lebanon

address: Abou Rizk Building, Abdel Wahab El Englizi Street Ashrafieh, Beirut Lebanon

review_text: Great sushi but terrible customer service. Got dirty looks from some of the staff reason must be cause we’re black. felt very much unwelcome and uncomfortable.

address: Lebanon street Street 61, Beirut Lebanon

review_text: The food was really delicious, the service too notch and the ambiance is really good! it was our first time there, for my wife’s birthday, i told them on the phone what i wanted and they did it without me telling them anything when we were there. they really make you feel at ease

address: Lebanon street Street 61, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: Great food the service the ambiance the food is simple but done right everything was perfect the ambiance live music good vibes and great selection on the menu

address: Lebanon street Street 61, Beirut Lebanon

Just Visited Beirut again and stopped by Germanos. Great place , i tasted the daily special on Friday , AN A AMAZING SAYADIEHA. local fish and rice dish with seaseme paste sauce .
still the same friendly atomsphere and everyboday knows everybody else, the're  all regualers and on a first name basis . straight away i felt at home . great food with even better prices

address: Rue Huvelin Near Yared Pharmacy, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: This place is just authentic. Everything you might expect in a Bistrot. The Family run business, the friendly smiling owners, the camaraderie and the lovely food. This place is a staple for this street and especially for all students at nearby St Joseph University. I cannot recommend this place enough

address: Rue Huvelin Near Yared Pharmacy, Beirut Lebanon
review_title: The neighbourhood cafe one needs in every city
review_text: Friendly hangout with simple yet good quality food. This is our Schwarze Pumpe (Berlin) of Beirut. A waterhole with mezze, salads and pizza. Also some daily dishes and all against a very fair price. We met some beautiful people here and we will be back.

address: Rue Huvelin Near Yared Pharmacy, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: Amazing Pizza and very friendly staff . Must try the Pepperoni and local speciality of Manaqueesh  Pastries with cheese , local Thyme and Spinach.

address: Rue Huvelin Near Yared Pharmacy, Beirut Lebanon
review_title: What though on hamely fare we dine,  A man’s a man for a' that
review_text:
If you'd like to taste well-prepared Lebanese fare, or the best pizza's and drink the only beer you'll find in Beirut - well-chilled Al-Maza, occasionally with a specialist lager - or a reasonable wine, whisky, whatever, then drop in to Germanos, around the corner from Peres Jesuites' church in Saifi, Tabaris, the start of Achrafieh - call it what you will!  Outside drinkers will be a mix of Lebanese and Celtic Englishmen (if that makes sense), often with other Europeans and an assortment of professions from retired writers and active journalists - the Times correspondent can be found here, to one of the most celebrated Lebanese Forces strategists ever to be glad it's all over now!  
    In short, the conversation is mesmerising and the food more than acceptable. Should cars pass, making too much noise with their engines or horns, you can join the locals in offering an opinion of the driver's heritage, though if there are children present, Caroline will ask that you keep such assessments civil, or to yourself.  On a Friday night you can take a break from 8.30 'til around 9.45 (so hurry back for a nightcap) to hear the Lebanese Philharmonic at the aforesaid house of prayer, acting as a fine concert venue, for free and excellently performed classical (in its broad sense) music - always with the best vocal and instrumental soloists. And yes, I did say free, gratuit, kostenlos. 
    So whether a tourist or unaware resident, go and sample the real Beirut for very reasonable prices, a chance to listen or show your ignorance and the challenge of  staying abstemious. It'll be fun.

address: Doumani street, Beirut 1100 Lebanon
review_text:
I tasted arak for the first time at Liza’s ;)
Here I found myself seated in the intimate courtyard of a historic homestead, jasmine flowering in front of me, a fountain splashing behind me and then this thrilling taste of subdued sweetness of an anise drink on a balmy spring day (1st May).
Waiters in ‘Beyrouth je t’aime ’ tee shirts were graced with trays and platters bearing an array of dishes starting with a beautiful artichoke salad tossed with garden-fresh peas,coriander,spring onion and ending with a joyful rice and caramel pudding. In between many leisurely shared dishes with my friends,Lebanese-style hospitality,there was time to peek inside the alluring Liza mansion and buy one of those ‘je t’aime’ tee shirts to express my reaction to Beirut -- a city that took me by surprise. Aside from communicating my reviews,the effortless artistry and elegance at Liza’s and astounding authenticity of Lebanon at large,left me speechless.

address: Doumani street, Beirut 1100 Lebanon
review_text: Liza in Beirut is a stunning fusion of elegance and authenticity, offering some of the finest Lebanese cuisine in the city. The standout dish of the evening was the beef manti—delicate dumplings packed with rich, savory flavors that left a lasting impression. Yet, beyond the food, it was Khalil who made the night truly exceptional. With his heartfelt warmth, genuine hospitality, and an instinctive care for every guest, he transformed the meal into an experience that felt less like dining out and more like being welcomed into a home. His thoughtful recommendations and effortless charm made every moment at Liza unforgettable.

address: Doumani street, Beirut 1100 Lebanon
review_title: Amazing dinner in an unparalleled location!
review_text: This restaurant gives you a cultural experience of Lebanon in one meal.  From the traditional food to the classical Lebanese house that is the setting, Liza encompasses the flavour and flair that is an unforgettable night out in Beirut.

address: Abdel Wahab Street, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: It really is the best poke place in Lebanon. I highly would recommend it. I went there with my friends and the poke were on an another level. Plus, the staff is very friendly and will explain everything to you in details if it’s your first time there. So overall I love this place and it’s just perfect if you feel like eating a rich poke bowl.

address: Abdel Wahab Street, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
Best poke place!! RECOMMENDED
The flaming shrimps as a starter. 
The salmon crunch is the best house creation you could taste.
And you should try the Japanese soft drink.
You won't regret it!!

address: Abdel Wahab Street, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: I ho to pick a poke 2 times per week and it's definitely the best poke place in Lebanon.much love to the Pick a Poke team !!!🥰

address: Abdel Wahab El Inglizi street, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: One of the best places to go to with friends. Great ambiance, delicious food and friendly service . Staff is helpful and accommodating and music is entertaining.

address: Abdel Wahab El Inglizi street, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
An excellent indoor outdoor Bar & Restaurant where you can enjoy food and drinks on the beats and music of DJ Najib. This guy can really mix music to turn your head more than the wine you drink.
Mgr Georges is also very friendly and efficient. The waitress was also excellent in terms of professional attention to clients and service dedication.
Went in by pure chance but will definitely come back.

address: Abdel Wahab El Inglizi street, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: I have travelled to a lot of countries and eaten in a lot of Mexican restaurants and I can assure this is one of the best I’ve ever tried. It may not be the most “authentic Mexican rest” but the nachos, quesadillas and salads were really really good. The bar and the drinks were also cool and there was a nice vibe

address: Abdel Wahab El Inglizi street, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
We sat outside and it was a lovely atmosphere and the food was delicious.
All staff were extremely friendly and attentive. I would definitely go back again.

address: Abdel Wahab El Inglizi street, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: This restaurant is in a STUNNING heritage building - in a pretty village type area of Achrefie - the food is excellent, the service is friendly, the frozen magaritas are AMAZING! AND BIG! Try the sizzling prawns, chicken wings and THE SEARED TUNA - best tuna I have ever had! Also fabulous salmon tartare! Terrace is so pretty! And atmosphere fabulous too!

address: Michel Chiha, Beirut 1111 Lebanon
review_text: If you want Lebanese traditional breakfast then you must visit La Foule at kantari area near where you used to be Harley bikes show room. The foul is tasty with the right amount of lemon, the fatteh is amazing, the zaater fatera is very rich and fulfilling and the oven meat slices with veggies is a must try. We had eggs cooked to our order and hummus was delicious. Overall highly recommended

address: Michel Chiha, Beirut 1111 Lebanon
review_title: A Riding Star in Lebanese Food
review_text:
In few words, La Foule would be a place where I could stay all day and have breakfast, lunch and dinner day after day after day...
The interior design, the hosting, the service and the unique simple Lebanese popular food elevated to a fourth dimension are a strike of a genius.
I can’t recommend a specific dish for each and every dish is an experience by itself.

address: Michel Chiha, Beirut 1111 Lebanon
review_text: Traditional Lebanese breakfast is associated with modest restaurants and set ups referred to locally as (Fawal - Foul maker) . La Foule is the only place you could have tasty foul and yummy traditional breakfast in a clean, fancy set up with top notch service. The plates are rich, tasty and nicely presented. Overall highly recommended

address: Michel Chiha, Beirut 1111 Lebanon
review_text: First time visit and the restaurant is really nice with a very pleasant ambiance and English speaking staff. I ordered the omlette and it came with a selection of side plates of vegetables, fabulous bread, humus, oilves etc. I also had fresh orange juice, coffee and a bottle of water and the total price was 11USD, great value. If you are in the area give it a try i will be back.

address: Michel Chiha, Beirut 1111 Lebanon
review_text: This restaurant is one of the best in Beirut to have a breakfast or brunch. The staff are friendly.The service is excellent.The food is amazing.I tasted fettah,Foul,Hommos,eggs with minced meat(awarma),eggs with sojok,lahme ras al asfour,Grilled halloum cheese and the special Thym pie.Every plate has its own taste and all are fantastic.I really recommended.

address: Myrtom House Bldg, Rue du Mexique, Kantari, Beirut 00000 Lebanon
review_text: A lovely restaurant in the heart of Beirut. I really enjoyed the Pad Thai and the dumplings. The server was attentive and the food came out quickly. They even offered a dessert on the house. I highly recommend this joint

address: Myrtom House Bldg, Rue du Mexique, Kantari, Beirut 00000 Lebanon
review_title: Major Let down, lacks proper taste and authenticity
review_text:
Went to this restaurant based on a friend’s recommendation saying that is the place to eat authentic Thai food in Beirut. First impression, the tables are outside on the street, no problem… we asked for the menu…was surprised that it is a mixed array of Thai, Indian, Chinese, Singaporean dishes. Prices are exaggerated and over priced. Asked for drinks menu since no drinks were listed on their menu (besides sifts drinks) …they don’t have one the server stated and then gave options orally…when asked about the price. , I was surprised when he said the price of white wine bottle is $28!!! Really !!!paying this much for a bottle of wine with tables on the street and no ambiance!!!
We ordered shrimps dumplings and chicken green curry . The dumplings are 4 small ones for $10 ….they gave no relation to what real dumplings should look or taste like , and I don’t think they were steamed since they were too mushy …I believe they were boiled.
With regards the chicken curry, it came in a bowl with so much sauce as if you are getting a soup dish! Also the sauce was too lumpy and grainy …not smooth.
This is not how Thai food should look or taste .
Any major issue I found was that they do not offer disposable chopsticks , they bring you a container with fires, knives, and string plastic chopsticks that get re washed and reused! Seriously!!! Unacceptable!
They do serve complimentary dessert …which some consider a plus

This place was a major let down …over priced, lacks authentic taste and food presentation

address: Myrtom House Bldg, Rue du Mexique, Kantari, Beirut 00000 Lebanon
review_text: Amazing experience one of a kind. Love the taste, menu and location. Must try while on BEY one of the best Asian food and experience. I like the street side seating at night.

address: Myrtom House Bldg, Rue du Mexique, Kantari, Beirut 00000 Lebanon
review_text: We went to this spot to eat Thai food.  Seating was outdoors with a few table for our large party. The menu as extensive from what I can remember. We ordered a few different things. The food was very tasty. Service was good. Only problem seating outside is cats running round!

address: Myrtom House Bldg, Rue du Mexique, Kantari, Beirut 00000 Lebanon
review_text: Love this place and the ambiance. If you are looking for sitting in the sidewalk style of Thai/ Asian  restaurant, then this is a must go to. We have so many favorites, that when it comes to ordering, we always over order and over eat! Fish cakes, padthai, green curry and their spinach salads are delicious. Everything gets prepared while you enjoy one of their own iced teas. On a hot day, I would recommend heading there for dinner. You might have to wait to be seated as it gets very busy and it is a small venue, but definitely worth it.

address: Justinian Street and Rue America Clemenceau Distric, Beirut Lebanon
review_title: This Grind Coffee has one of the most unique and creative designs I have ever seen in a coffee shop...
review_text:
The Grind Coffee House is a great new coffee house located in the Clemenceau District of Beirut just across from the Trad Hospital and the famous Dar El Nimer for Arts & Culture. It is near both Justinian Street and Rue America in this very trendy neighborhood.
This Grind Coffee has one of the most unique and creative designs I have ever seen in a coffee shop as it is completely made of natural wood and the seating is all outside on a nice wooden deck. 
They are serving a very special Spanish blended coffee and have many tea labels to select from... They are also serving light food items and great tasting bake goods. The service is very fast and their prices are competitive for the Beirut market.
On the seating deck, they also have several board games and many paperback books to entertain oneself during your time when you are enjoying your coffee or tea!
If you really want a change in your coffee shop experience, I really suggest that you try the Grind Coffee shop in the Clemenceau District of Beirut. Trust me you will not be sorry…

address: Spears Street facing the Lebanese Red Cross, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: This tiny shop provides the best crepes in Lebanon. I might sound a bit exaggerating but boy how satisfied I was after my visit to 101 Gourmet. For our Saturday night treat I headed to this place after hearing positive comments regarding the crepes they make. It's a tiny shop with only one crepe board machine. The employee was polite and helpful. He suggested I take their special pizza crepe and the white hershey's with lotus spread and digestive biscuit crashed chunks, I also took one additional crepe containing Nutella and halawa. For a start I noticed that the board on which the crepes are made is rectangular not round like the ones usually seen at crepes parlors. The employee does not spread the crepe liquid mix by hands but instead he puts it in a sort of container connected to the board and simply passes it above.  You won't find those crepes the gooey empty edges as they don't fold their crepes the usual way. The crepe dough isn't unpleasantly sweet and it's very well cooked having that lovely brown golden color. All the crepes we got were amazingly tasty. I noticed that for the Pizza crepe they use Barilla special pizza sauce and they add good quality Mozzarella cheese they add to that black olives, mushrooms and corn (which they make sure to remove the water from) and here you go !!! As for the sweet crepes they were marvelous especially the one recommended by the employee (white hershey's with lotus spread and digestive biscuit crashed chunks) !!! The crepes prices are cheaper here than the ones at the competitors. It's surely my new favorite Crepes spot in town.

address: Spears Street facing the Lebanese Red Cross, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: It's a small spot for quick bite and takeaways. I go for the Frankfurter hotdog sandwich and the delicious crepes. Mozzarella & kinder and Hershey's crepes are the tastiest. Fast service and affordable prices

address: Spears Street facing the Lebanese Red Cross, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: Received a hot dog sandwich delivery from 101 Gourmet to work. The packaging was decent and the sandwich arrived in good condition. Overall it tasted great with adequately cooked hot dog. The price is excellent relative to portion size and taste.

address: Spears Street facing the Lebanese Red Cross, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
Best hotdogs in town !!! 
The crepes are to die for... Especially the Nutella ones...
If baked potatoes are your thing they are one of the few places in town that do them 
Very clean place, friendly staff ! 
And they deliver !!!!!!

address: Spears Street facing the Lebanese Red Cross, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: Best crepe ever... So yummy. It's a must once u r in Beirut.  Heard so much about the hotdog; will try very soon. Friendly staff

address: Rue Hussein Beyhum Zoukak El-Blate, Beirut 1107 Lebanon
review_text: I ordered some lahmadjun at the Ichkhanian bakery for delivery at their Zokak al-Blatt location. I had the classic lahmadjun and the one with pomegranate molasses. Both were excellent. The dow was delicious, and the minced meat was very tasty, mixed with chopped tomatoes and red bell peppers, with the right amount of spices. This bakery has existed for decades and I was happy to discover that it was living up to its reputation as one of the best lahmadjun places in Beirut, if not the best.

review_title: I visit the new branch in

address: Rue Hussein Beyhum Zoukak El-Blate, Beirut 1107 Lebanon
review_text: For those of you wanting a traditional and delicious Lebanese/Armenian food look no further. I did not allow my friends have the hotel breakfast on the morning we visited and even though they were initially perplexed as to why...they soon found out it was very wise to do so. The lachmacun is cooked in a traditional oven and right in front of your very eyes. The best we've had, even the vegetarian version was great. It works out just under $2 per piece. Expect to eat on the street or just take it with you. it's not easy to find, our taxi driver did not know it ..lucky I had the phone number.

address: Nicolas Rebeiz Al Achkar Building, Talaat Joumblat, Facing CMC Hospital, Beirut Lebanon
review_title: A beautiful way to spend one’s last evening in Beirut
review_text:
We decided to have Italian food for our last dinner in Beirut. We chose this place as its near to our hotel.
The restaurant is small. Just five tables on the ground floor . Upstairs, i think there is a kitchen, no idea about seating.
The host Samir is genuinely friendly and passionate about the food. I could not decide so he gave me a few options. Anyway, we chose veg gnocchi, a spaghetti bolognese and a meat lasagna. Absolutely divine and when teamed up with white Heritage sweet wine, it was an unbeatable combination.
Food was relatively expensive but outstanding. Well worth the cost. 
As we asked for a cab and said it was our last night, Samir whipped off the table cloth and said that foreigners who visit his place are requested to follow the tradition! He made us write a comment in our language on the wooden table top. 

A great way to leave Beirut with wonderful memories. Thank you, Samir.

address: Nicolas Rebeiz Al Achkar Building, Talaat Joumblat, Facing CMC Hospital, Beirut Lebanon

address: Beirut Lebanon
review_title: Disappointing service and poor handling of customer concerns. Management should act.
review_text:
Our experience at this restaurant in Dbayeh was disappointing due to multiple service-related issues.

Upon arrival, the valet was sitting in a parked car and did not assist with parking. After waiting approximately 10 minutes, we parked our car on the road ourselves. Despite this, the valet later approached us requesting parking fees without providing a parking ticket!

Later in the evening, the valet came to our table inside the restaurant and began arguing with us about the parking fee in an unprofessional and impolite manner. Restaurant staff did not intervene. Although we paid the requested amount, no parking ticket was issued! 

When settling the restaurant bill, we paid using $100 bills and expected to receive change. The waiter did not return the change! We waited and waited but he did not bring the change . Only after calling him back did he bring the change, without any apology. Tipping should always be at the customer’s discretion and should not be assumed.

We positively raised these concerns with the manager, including:
	•	The valet parking issue
	•	Food quality and presentation
	•	The handling of the bill and change

While the manager listened, no corrective action or resolution was offered.

We hope management reviews these issues seriously and takes steps to improve service standards and customer experience.

address: Rafic Salloum Palladium Building, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
Our bill was approximately 230,000 L.L for one good plate, one plat with unwanted HAIR that I had to sit through and eat, and two glasses of wine that we were FORCED to drink.

Read Below!

Last night, my friend and I went to Gavi to celebrate his birthday. Upon arriving, we were seated and everything was going fine. We ordered one Mushroom Spaghetti as well as a Fruit de Marre pasta (seafood pasta).

Minutes later, two wine glasses are delivered to our table without us asking for them, and by another waiter. So we called our original waitress and informed her of the error, that’s when she insisted that we taste the wine glasses without taking no for an answer. My friend had a sip and complimented the taste, and again she insisted on us having them.

We were grateful that she was going to offer the wine glasses to us as a form of apology for the error.

The first plate to be brought to the table was the risotto which tasted delicious ! However, when the Fruit de marre was brought and the waitress poured for each of us a plate, I noticed A LONG STRAND OF HAIR in my plate !!! I called for her and showed her the hair, she apologized and said she would take it to the kitchen to “FIX” it, she did so while leaving my friends plate on the table, one that contained food from that same big plate from which the hair was poured with the food into my plate.

We were sitting outside and right infant of us was a glass that separates the inside area, thats where the manager was sitting. We saw her explain to him what happened and him not reacting in anyway because he was on his phone. She then went to the kitchen with the plate, and someone from the kitchen went outside to talk to the manager, again with no reactions from him whatsoever.

Later on, the waitress brought back the same plate minus the hair of course, with the same portion as she took it ( she had already poured my friend a plate from before she took it) and she claimed that the plate was new when it was very obvious that the plate only had the portion she took with her, and was served less warm than that she took back to the kitchen !!!!! ( THEY MADE US PAY FOR IT)

But thats not all, we were charged an extra 75,000 L.L for the wine !!!! The same wine they insisted on us having that was brought to our table by error!!! We asked for her to remove the wine because THEY OFFERED IT TO US! So she ended up removing one glass of the two. And we left.

We caught our server looking over at our table inappropriately for the setting, gossiping with his colleague, and laughing. It’s a surprise as this never happened before and frankly this will be the last time we will come.

address: Minet el Hosn Paladium building, Down Town, Beirut Lebanon
review_title: Come as a group of at least four. Order a little of everything.
review_text:
Beirut is noticeably on the mend after the 2020 port explosion that devasted the city leaving hundred’s dead. With a favourable terroir, land and sea, and some decades of French influence, you can look forward to great food.

There’s a simplicity in great Japanese restaurants that adheres to the adage, same, same, but different. The décor is very simple and no less elegant and contemporary for it. Located between Zaitunay Bay, Downtown and the political district, it services a demanding clientele.

That the menu has no prices on the printed version might make a first-time visitor uneasy, but worry not. Due to currency instability, which has led to the beginning of nationwide dollarisation, pricing can be fluid in Lebanese Lira. 

There is a QR code that will give you current menu pricing in US Dollars. This is very common and should not worry you. You will be far too interested in the fine range of offerings, the wines every bit as appealing.

Sea salted edamame for the immediately famished enjoyed, it was time to place both feet firmly inside.

Fresh salmon maki put a smile on my wife’s face, pre and post, much like a child getting a small ice cream cone, albeit this one in wrapped glorious dark seaweed.

Ordered from the salad menu, odd given the greens were really tasty garnish, were two helpings of crispy spicy salmon. Sashimi salmon in popcorn popping panko crumb and a light late lingering spice. If we were ordering in stages, I’m not sure we’d have eaten anything else.

Tuna and salmon sashimi were on point, fresh ginger and wasabi likewise.

Three separate sushi platters arrived next. There were a few of us. I’m not sure I’ve seen a mango ura maki before. The colour was joyful and like its neighbours disappeared systemically into us happy diners.

I opted for the wagyu foie gras, ordered and served bleu. Merci chef. Both were similarly butchered for their plating, served as two fractured asteroids one atop the other. Holding it together was a thuddingly thickened teriyaki sauce. From the nose up to my eyes, everything was alight. 

It cut like butter and melted similarly in all our mouths. As I write this an hour later, its ghost still happily haunts my mouth.

The service is good, efficient, the waiters know their menu and can talk for it.

I don’t know when I’ll be back in Beirut again, but if I am, I’ll dine here at least once.

address: Saifi Village, Beirut Lebanon
review_title: Do not judge a book by its cover!
review_text:
This is a really nice place: food is just perfect to get out of the “Lebanese routine” that you fall into in Beirut. 
The location is great in this little, shady square in the Saifi village, the service was attentive.
The rating would be better if not for their attitude towards tourists (and tourists looking people). So the place was empty (and still with outside tables empty when we left) but we couldn’t be seated outside! Of course every rich and good looking person that arrived was welcomed outside (I can tell you that there were more Hermes bags in the restaurant that in the Hermes shop itself)....obviously not looking the part enough! However my Black Amex changed their attitude at paying the bill...do not judge a book from the cover! So I will not be running back...I cannot stand attitude!

address: Saifi Village, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: The simplicity and what seems like militant dedication to great ingredients sets Paname apart from all the so called contemporary restaurants of Beirut. Carrots, artichokes, asparagus, black rice are treated with the same reverence as Blue Fin Tuna, delivering not just photogenic plating but incredibly balanced tastes and textures that were music to my palates. In the hands of Paname, sea creatures deliver the unique and complex taste of the ocean without even the hint of fishy smell. Do not let the ambitious menu intimidate you, the service aims to please any cravings, requests, or food fantasies. When the sides of a dish that was 86 for the day appealed to me, it was generously offered with another fish. No ego at Paname, just good food.

address: Saifi Village, Beirut Lebanon

address: Miele Building, Downtown Beirut 6&7th Floor, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
We arrived on a Wednesday night at 7h for happy hour to Pre Beirut and we were received by a rude security guard on steroids, telling us where do you think you are going like that? In a rude tone, I asked him how does he allow himself to talk to people like that? He said if we are here without a reservation we cannot go in especially that we are 3 guys, we just turned our backs and left the building without going in after such a reception from an illiterate security guard.
We directly called the number of Pre Beirut and a certain girl answers, so we explained to her the situation asking to talk with management, she said the security guard was not allowed to do this and its not his job but she said she won't do anything about it and seemed totally careless.
We were stunned by their lack of customer service as if they are acting like that to scare customers off on purpose.
This was the first time we face such insolent behavior from staff/security and it shows the poor quality of service you will be getting in this place. No wonder it is not working nor popular and bad reviews are pouring like rain.
My advice to you, avoid like plague.

address: Miele Building, Downtown Beirut 6&7th Floor, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: If you are anyone with some class or self respect I wouldn't go there. I live in the USA and I go to lebanon to have a good time and enjoy myself i called the club to see if they had any reservations available and they told me they were full but hey could squeeze us in at that one table. They said it would be $40 minimum charge. When I got there it felt like I was in a crack house. There were no lights, it seemed as though the people there were high, the place was empty there was maybe 10 guys who were on some sort of drugs. It wasn't a place for couples and I went with my boyfriend and two other couples. As a girl we all felt really uncomfortable. We were lied to by the staff saying it was full when it was clearly empty. I came to Lebanon for two weeks and I waisted one of my Friday nights there because it was one of be most miserable times of my life. If I could give a negative rating I would.

address: Miele Building, Downtown Beirut 6&7th Floor, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
If I was to rate the welcome and service, I would put ZERO. But to be fair for the Dj and the lovely music I will put half the grade.

What a shame, I called to inquire about reservation, the guy told me to be as early as 9:30 to guarantee spot. When we arrived, the bouncers at the main entrance of building started interrogating us (three ladies) as if we are at the prison entrance! Stuff like who are you here for?! and why are you here that early, until he called them to check if they are ready to receive?! And so we were granted the permission to cross the borders.
We arrived at the 6th floor where the staff was hardly ready. No welcome whatsoever, we sat at the bar and the waitress as well as another waiter who both lack training, approached us. They said that they don't have drinks menu.
We ordered:
- One  Moscow mule that came with an average taste and no cucumber (not available)
-Another friend ordered rosé yet the waiter poured her white wine; she had to ask for him to replace it
- Seven up diet that was initially  served in a plastic cup until i asked her why not the normal glass one?!
They have a policy where you are requested either to pay each drink straight or to deposit the visa card until departure. Maybe they had few bad experiences. But the way they ask for it is not really appropriate; it was as if looking at the customer in his eye doubting him and unconsciously accusing him of theft!
 
The enjoyable thing is the Music, the age range is mid -late twenties.

I think that they have a potential with a beautiful location, they need an adequate training of the staff, a less suspicious attitude but most importantly more respectful bouncers.
P.S:  Don't drag people early if you are not ready to receive.

address: Moukhallassiya Street Saifi Village, Beirut Lebanon
review_title: Expensive hipster food with no soul.
review_text:
Unbelievably expensive.
Hipster staff care more about seeming cool than actually doing their job.

The concept of farm or market to table restaurants is a wonderful idea. However, when the farm of the meat you are selling is in Australia, it really loses its authenticity. The restaurant is very ‘hipster’ and is clearly full of young  Lebanese expats or student who live outside of Lebanon but have returned for their summer holidays-rich kids. As someone living in Manchester, I am used to expensive meals, however I was blown away by the prices in this restaurant- $27 for a burger is insane! 

Now onto the service, the staff look great, in fact, they seemed more interested in themselves than the customers. No one explained to us that we had to choose our own meat before ordering, or the fact you then pay extra for them to actually cook the meal. 

My honest advice is:
lower your prices 
Get staff to explain the concept and guide customers through the market
Lebanon is a great country so celebrate its produce rather than getting your meat from Australia.

address: Saifi Suites Hotel, Saifi, Downtown Beirut, Beirut Lebanon
review_title: If you plan to visit this restaurant PLEASE read this review first!!!
review_text:
Currently having dinner in this restaurant on April 9th 2024. 
I just couldn’t resist writing this review while sitting there to show how much I am furious about the very bad service they have…first of all …our waiter ,a Lebanese guy with dark short hair and goatee has terrible customer service manners. We try to order from him and he has such a grumpy look and barely waits for you to finish your sentence…as if he is too busy and we are wasting his pre IOU’s time. …service is terrible…we have to continuously ask them to take a look at our empty wine glasses to refill them. 
They bring appetisers without serving utensils. We have to call waiters to remove empty glasses and dishes from the table. 
We asked for a bottle of wine and bottle of water. They brought the water bottle without water glasses. 
No salt and pepper shakers on the table. 
I ordered the beef tenderloin and my son ordered the same. The attached picture shows his dish. My dish actually had 2 thin pieces of steak put on top of each to show as if it was s thick piece of tenderloin! 
Vegetables were dry and super all dente!!!
I am embarrassed to be writing this review while sitting there since I am invited for dinner…but this has been such s terrible experience …from ALL aspects.

address: Gemmayzeh, Rue du Liban Street, Zoghbi Bldg., GF Next To Pharmacie Sainte Lucie, Beirut 0000 Lebanon
review_title: Hip and very laid back with great music and a great crowd!
review_text:
One of the coziest bars in Beirut, with laid back people and friendly staff, where drinks are affordable and the food is great. A lot of locals, a lot of foreigners, this is a place where you can go and mingle with anyone, or just listen to the coolest music. They change DJs every night, so the music is pretty diverse.
Also a great place for food (try the QUINOA burger!) or just a coffee. It is quite during the day, so it's a good place to work (free wifi). Would definitely recommend for any visitor wanting a taste of the hip Lebanese night life.

address: Boutros Dagher Street, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:

The decor is stunning, featuring authentic, handcrafted pieces made in the family workshop, which give the place a special and personal touch. The atmosphere is warm and friendly, with an amazing team.

It’s a place that exudes authenticity and makes you feel right at home from the moment you step in. Even though it’s not on a main street, that only makes it more of a treasure waiting to be discovered. I wholeheartedly recommend it for anyone looking to enjoy a wonderful time in the company of kind and truly exceptional people.

review_text: 10 over 10 is not enough. Amazing how a small place can offer such great desserts. Cookies are mouthwatering. Cupcakes, cookie dough Oreo are a must try. Very vibrant, positive,and friendly staff. Prices are reasonable.

address: Amir Omar Street, Beirut 60170 Lebanon
review_title: It's not Just Better It's the Best
review_text: Finally a bakery in Lebanon that produces excellent/super tasty cookies, cinnamon rolls, brownies and many other delicious treats exactly the real American way like they should be done. Bravo. I have almost lost hope in getting decent cookies after tasting several bakeries productions in Beirut. Until I read great reviews about this place. I went there with no high expectations but luckily I was amazed. It's a small cute little shop in a clam area in Beirut. The goodies are very well displayed. The lady who helped me was friendly and informative. I grabbed one cinnamon roll, one kinder filled cookie, one caramel (Twix) brownie and finally one cake ball filled with Nutella. I can safely say that all what I have purchased were amazingly tasty. It's clear that this bakery uses only premium quality ingredients and it has the real American know how. The cookie is big, chewy and generously filled. The cake ball filled with Nutella and dipped in chocolate is out of this world. The brownie was rich and moist. Cinnamon roll was fresh and full of flavors. Portions are more than fair and the prices for those jewels are so reasonable. I so recommend this bakery.

address: Assurex Bldg, Abdel Hamid Ghandour Street Downtown Beirut, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
Beirut is a little tricky as far as location is concerned. This restaurant is somehwat hidden away, but don't let that put you off. Is is easy to locate on google maps or on Allo taxi. It is small, boutique and very, very friendly. 

So much for location.

But here is the kicker. The staff are attentive without being overbearing. Arabic, French and English are spoken with equivalent fluency. There are brilliant starters and coffee and desserts are available. But the real deal here are the steaks. 

Yes; you will pay for what you get; It is not cheap, but when it comes it is sublime. Or better. I will confess I had an Argentinian 200g fillet and chips. It was quite simply wonderful. 

I was in Beirut for two weeks and would (and will) go back here to get a real, quality and carefully well-cooked steak which you could compare with anything you could get in London, Munich, Sydney or Cape Town.

LBP, USD and mainstream credit cards are welcome (with a + for international credit cards)

If  you are hankering for a clearly wonderful beef meat meal with 100% quality, then this is your place.

address: Ghandour Building, Minaa El Hosn, Downtown, Beirut Lebanon

Felicitations for Mrs. Frida
